---
title: "Xenium Analysis with Harmony and SCINA"
author: "Shah"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.width = 8,
  fig.height = 6
)
```
# 1. Load Required Packages
```{r load-packages}
library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)
library(cowplot)
library(ggplot2)
library(arrow)
library(XeniumSpatialAnalysis)
library(harmony)
library(edgeR)
library(remotes)
library(patchwork)
library(clustree)
library(Matrix)
library(hdf5r)
library(circlize)
library(ComplexHeatmap)
```
# 2. Define File Paths
```{r set-paths}
setwd("~/labs/lamantia/Shah/Xenium Files")
home <- "~/labs/lamantia/Shah/Xenium Files/LD-WT-P5_NAC 9.10.25"
save.location <- "~/labs/lamantia/Shah/Xenium Files/saveRDS"

```

```{r read all_samples_before_sct.rds merged file caution !!!! no replicate metadata}

# Read saved RDS, add metadata, merge into all_samples
setwd(save.location)
all_samples <- readRDS(file.path(save.location, "all_samples_before_SCT.rds"))

```

```{r add replicate labels to each section}

# load one metadata file, e.g., WT
meta_WT <- read.csv("~/labs/lamantia/Shah/Xenium Files/Replicates_Metadata/WT_cells_with_sections.csv")

# create a lookup table with cleaned IDs
meta_WT$cell_id_clean <- meta_WT$cell_id  # copy original
names(meta_WT)[names(meta_WT) == "cell_id"] <- "cell_id_raw"  # rename for clarity

# prepare Seurat IDs without suffix
seurat_ids_clean <- sub("_[0-9]+$", "", colnames(all_samples))

# find matching cells (keep indices)
matches <- match(meta_WT$cell_id_clean, seurat_ids_clean)

# subset only where match exists
valid_idx <- which(!is.na(matches))
meta_WT_matched <- meta_WT[valid_idx, ]


# make sure these columns exist in the metadata
all_samples$section <- NA
all_samples$animal_id <- NA

# now safely add metadata
all_samples$section[ matches[valid_idx] ]   <- meta_WT_matched$section
all_samples$animal_id[ matches[valid_idx] ] <- meta_WT_matched$animal_id

table(all_samples$genotype, all_samples$animal_id, useNA = "ifany")



# LD
meta_LD <- read.csv("~/labs/lamantia/Shah/Xenium Files/Replicates_Metadata/LD_cells_with_sections.csv")
meta_LD$cell_id_clean <- meta_LD$cell_id
names(meta_LD)[names(meta_LD) == "cell_id"] <- "cell_id_raw"
matches <- match(meta_LD$cell_id_clean, seurat_ids_clean)
valid_idx <- which(!is.na(matches))
meta_LD_matched <- meta_LD[valid_idx, ]
all_samples$section[ matches[valid_idx] ]   <- meta_LD_matched$section
all_samples$animal_id[ matches[valid_idx] ] <- meta_LD_matched$animal_id

# LD_NAC
meta_LD_NAC <- read.csv("~/labs/lamantia/Shah/Xenium Files/Replicates_Metadata/LD_NAC_cells_with_sections.csv")
meta_LD_NAC$cell_id_clean <- meta_LD_NAC$cell_id
names(meta_LD_NAC)[names(meta_LD_NAC) == "cell_id"] <- "cell_id_raw"
matches <- match(meta_LD_NAC$cell_id_clean, seurat_ids_clean)
valid_idx <- which(!is.na(matches))
meta_LD_NAC_matched <- meta_LD_NAC[valid_idx, ]
all_samples$section[ matches[valid_idx] ]   <- meta_LD_NAC_matched$section
all_samples$animal_id[ matches[valid_idx] ] <- meta_LD_NAC_matched$animal_id

# WT_NAC
meta_WT_NAC <- read.csv("~/labs/lamantia/Shah/Xenium Files/Replicates_Metadata/WT_NAC_cells_with_sections.csv")
meta_WT_NAC$cell_id_clean <- meta_WT_NAC$cell_id
names(meta_WT_NAC)[names(meta_WT_NAC) == "cell_id"] <- "cell_id_raw"
matches <- match(meta_WT_NAC$cell_id_clean, seurat_ids_clean)
valid_idx <- which(!is.na(matches))
meta_WT_NAC_matched <- meta_WT_NAC[valid_idx, ]
all_samples$section[ matches[valid_idx] ]   <- meta_WT_NAC_matched$section
all_samples$animal_id[ matches[valid_idx] ] <- meta_WT_NAC_matched$animal_id

```

```{r remove cells that were mismatch: loose cells between the sections etc}
# Current mismatches
mismatch <- all_samples@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(animal_id)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    (genotype == "WT" & !animal_id %in% paste0("WT_", 1:5)) |
    (genotype == "LD" & !animal_id %in% paste0("LD_", 1:5)) |
    (genotype == "WT_NAC" & !animal_id %in% paste0("WT_NAC_", 1:3)) |
    (genotype == "LD_NAC" & !animal_id %in% paste0("LD_NAC_", 1:5))
  )
nrow(mismatch)

# Cells missing metadata
na_cells <- all_samples@meta.data %>% 
  dplyr::filter(is.na(animal_id) | is.na(section))
nrow(na_cells)
table(na_cells$genotype)

cells_to_remove <- unique(c(rownames(mismatch), rownames(na_cells)))
all_samples <- subset(all_samples, cells = setdiff(colnames(all_samples), cells_to_remove))

# open a PDF device before the plot
pdf(file.path(save.location, "QC_VlnPlot_by_replicate.pdf"),
    width = 14, height = 8)

VlnPlot(
  all_samples,
  features = c("nCount_Xenium", "nFeature_Xenium"),
  group.by = "animal_id",
  pt.size = 0.1
)

dev.off()  # close the PDF


#saveRDS(all_samples, file.path(save.location, "all_samples_with_clean_replicate_metadata_before_sct.rds"))


```

```{r load merged all_samples rds with replicate metadata and filter 22q11 genes}

# For RStudio, use multisession instead of multicore
plan("multisession", workers = 12)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 150 * 1024^3)  # 150GB

# Read saved RDS, add metadata, merge into all_samples
setwd(save.location)
all_samples <- readRDS(file.path(save.location, "all_samples_with_clean_replicate_metadata_before_sct.rds"))

DefaultAssay(all_samples) <- "Xenium"

all_samples <- SCTransform(
  all_samples,
  assay = "Xenium",
  method = "glmGamPoi",
  vars.to.regress = "nCount_Xenium",
  verbose = TRUE
)

```

```{r run pca and umap clustering excluding 22q11 genes}

# For RStudio, use multisession instead of multicore
plan("multisession", workers = 12)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 150 * 1024^3)  # 150GB


x22q11_genes <- c("Ranbp1","Dgcr8","Cdc45","Trmt2a","Comt",
                  "Tango2","Mrpl40","Slc25a1","Zdhhc8",
                  "Txnrd2","Hira","Prodh")

available_22q11 <- intersect(x22q11_genes, VariableFeatures(all_samples))
VariableFeatures(all_samples) <- setdiff(VariableFeatures(all_samples), available_22q11)

all_samples <- RunPCA(all_samples, npcs = 50, verbose = FALSE)
ElbowPlot(all_samples)


# suggest PCs capturing ~90% variance
pca_var <- all_samples[["pca"]]@stdev^2
pca_var_perc <- pca_var / sum(pca_var) * 100
suggested_pcs <- which(cumsum(pca_var_perc) >= 80)[1]
cat("Suggested PCs:", suggested_pcs, "\n")

all_samples <- RunHarmony(
  object = all_samples,
  group.by.vars = "animal_id",
  dims.use = 1:21,
  verbose = TRUE
)


all_samples <- RunUMAP(all_samples, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:21)
all_samples <- FindNeighbors(all_samples, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:21)


saveRDS(all_samples, file.path(save.location, "no_22q_all_samples_with_clean_replicate_metadata_SCT_Harmony_before_findcluster_res0.1.rds"))


#all_samples <- readRDS(file.path(save.location, "no_22q_all_samples_with_clean_replicate_metadata_SCT_Harmony_before_findcluster_res0.1.rds"))

all_samples <- FindClusters(all_samples, resolution = 0.1)


any(x22q11_genes %in% rownames(Loadings(all_samples[["pca"]])))


saveRDS(all_samples, file.path(save.location, "no_22q_all_samples_with_clean_replicate_metadata_SCT_Harmony_after_findcluster_res0.1.rds"))


all_samples <- readRDS(file.path(save.location, "no_22q_all_samples_with_clean_replicate_metadata_SCT_Harmony_after_findcluster_res0.1.rds"))








DimPlot(all_samples, reduction = "umap")

# Light, cyan-inspired color scheme
genotype_colors <- c(
  "WT" = "#A6CEE3",      # Bright cyan
  "LD" = "#FB9A99",      # Coral red
  "LD_NAC" = "#FDBF6F",  # Sky blue
  "WT_NAC" = "#FFFF99"   # Seafoam green
)

# Create the UMAP plot
p <- DimPlot(all_samples, 
             group.by = "genotype",
             cols = genotype_colors,
             pt.size = 1.2) +
     ggtitle("UMAP - All Genotypes") +
     theme_light() +
     theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14))

print(p)

# Save the plot
ggsave("simple_genotypes_umap_pastel_theme.pdf", p, width = 10, height = 8)


# Enhanced version with clean axis lines only (no grid)
p_enhanced <- DimPlot(all_samples, 
                     group.by = "genotype",
                     cols = genotype_colors,
                     pt.size = 1.5,  # Slightly larger points
                     label = FALSE,
                     repel = TRUE) +
     ggtitle("UMAP - All Genotypes") +
     theme_minimal() +
     theme(
         plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
         legend.text = element_text(size = 12),
         legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
         axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
         axis.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
         # Remove grid lines
         panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
         panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
         # Keep axis lines
         axis.line = element_line(color = "black", size = 0.5),
         # Optional: add a subtle border around the plot
         panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA, size = 0.5)
     ) +
     guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 4)))  # Larger legend points

print(p_enhanced)

# Save as high-quality PDF
ggsave("simple_genotypes_umap_cyan_theme_clean.pdf", 
       p_enhanced, 
       width = 12, 
       height = 9,
       dpi = 600)

```

```{r join layers, normalize, and scale find cluster markers}

plan("multisession", workers = 12)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 150 * 1024^3)  # 150GB

DefaultAssay(all_samples) <- "Xenium"
all_samples <- JoinLayers(all_samples)
all_samples <- NormalizeData(all_samples, normalization.method = "LogNormalize")
all_samples <- ScaleData(all_samples)


# Find markers with slightly relaxed parameters
AllMarkers <- FindAllMarkers(
    all_samples,
    assay = "Xenium",
    only.pos = TRUE,
    min.pct = 0.25,           # Reduced from 0.25
    logfc.threshold = 0.25,   # Reduced from 0.25
    test.use = "wilcox"      # Use Wilcoxon test
)

# save for reference
write.csv(AllMarkers, file.path(save.location, "new_strict_all_samples_no2211_Xenium_FindAllMarkers_res0.1_dims21.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r Plot cluster with chosen colors and labels using FinaAllmarker identity markers}
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(plyr)

# -------------------------------------------------
# define color palette (balanced + distinct)
# -------------------------------------------------
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # Deep-layer excitatory
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # Upper-layer excitatory
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Interneurons (Lhx6+)
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / vascular
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / immature oligos
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / macrophages
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 corticothalamic PNs
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# cluster identity labels
# -------------------------------------------------
cluster_labels <- c(
  "0" = "L5/6 Excitatory PNs",
  "1" = "Striatal GABAergic (MSNs)",
  "2" = "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs",
  "3" = "Astrocytes / Radial Glia",
  "4" = "Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
  "5" = "Endothelial / Vascular",
  "6" = "OPCs / Immature Oligos",
  "7" = "Microglia / Macrophages",
  "8" = "L2/3 IT PNs",
  "9" = "L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs"
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# map cluster numbers to names
# -------------------------------------------------
all_samples$celltype <- plyr::mapvalues(
  as.character(all_samples$seurat_clusters),
  from = names(cluster_labels),
  to = cluster_labels
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# PDF 1 — numbered clusters with custom colors
# -------------------------------------------------
pdf("UMAP_no22q11_clusters_colored.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
DimPlot(
  all_samples,
  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
  cols = cluster_colors,
  label = TRUE,
  repel = TRUE
) +
  ggtitle("Clusters (0–9)") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
dev.off()

# -------------------------------------------------
# PDF 2 — labeled clusters (same colors)
# -------------------------------------------------
pdf("UMAP_no22q11_clusters_labeled.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
DimPlot(
  all_samples,
  group.by = "celltype",
  cols = cluster_colors,
  label = TRUE,
  repel = TRUE
) +
  ggtitle("Clusters by Cell Type") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
dev.off()

```

```{r even better umap plots with multipe pdf outputs}
library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(plyr)

# -------------------------------------------------
# define color palette (balanced + distinct)
# -------------------------------------------------
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # Deep-layer excitatory
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # Upper-layer excitatory
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Interneurons (Lhx6+)
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / vascular
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / immature oligos
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / macrophages
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 corticothalamic PNs
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# cluster identity labels
# -------------------------------------------------
cluster_labels <- c(
  "0" = "L5/6 Excitatory PNs",
  "1" = "Striatal GABAergic (MSNs)",
  "2" = "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs",
  "3" = "Astrocytes / Radial Glia",
  "4" = "Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
  "5" = "Endothelial / Vascular",
  "6" = "OPCs / Immature Oligos",
  "7" = "Microglia / Macrophages",
  "8" = "L2/3 IT PNs",
  "9" = "L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs"
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# map cluster numbers to names
# -------------------------------------------------
all_samples$celltype <- plyr::mapvalues(
  as.character(all_samples$seurat_clusters),
  from = names(cluster_labels),
  to = cluster_labels
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# Check available genotypes in metadata
# -------------------------------------------------
print("Available genotypes:")
print(unique(all_samples$genotype))

# -------------------------------------------------
# Create ImageDimPlot for each genotype
# -------------------------------------------------

# Option 1: If you have spatial coordinates and want spatial plots
# Replace 'slice' with your actual slice name if using spatial data
pdf("ImageDimPlot_by_genotype_clusters.pdf", width = 12, height = 8)

# Plot by numeric clusters for each genotype
if("genotype" %in% names(all_samples@meta.data)) {
  # Get unique genotypes
  genotypes <- unique(all_samples$genotype)
  
  for(geno in genotypes) {
    # Subset by genotype
    geno_subset <- subset(all_samples, subset = genotype == geno)
    
    # Plot with numeric clusters
    p1 <- DimPlot(geno_subset, 
                  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
                  cols = cluster_colors,
                  label = TRUE,
                  repel = TRUE) +
      ggtitle(paste("Genotype:", geno, "- Clusters (0-9)")) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
    
    # Plot with cell type labels
    p2 <- DimPlot(geno_subset,
                  group.by = "celltype",
                  cols = cluster_colors,
                  label = TRUE,
                  repel = TRUE) +
      ggtitle(paste("Genotype:", geno, "- Cell Types")) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
    
    # Print both plots
    print(p1)
    print(p2)
    
    # Also create a combined plot showing cell distribution
    p3 <- DimPlot(geno_subset,
                  group.by = "celltype",
                  cols = cluster_colors) +
      ggtitle(paste("Genotype:", geno, "- Cell Type Distribution")) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
    print(p3)
  }
}

dev.off()

# -------------------------------------------------
# Alternative: Faceted plots by genotype
# -------------------------------------------------
pdf("ImageDimPlot_faceted_by_genotype.pdf", width = 16, height = 10)

# Faceted plot by genotype - numeric clusters
p_facet_num <- DimPlot(all_samples,
                       group.by = "seurat_clusters",
                       split.by = "genotype",
                       cols = cluster_colors,
                       ncol = 2) +  # Adjust ncol based on number of genotypes
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

# Faceted plot by genotype - cell types
p_facet_cell <- DimPlot(all_samples,
                        group.by = "celltype",
                        split.by = "genotype",
                        cols = cluster_colors,
                        ncol = 2) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

print(p_facet_num)
print(p_facet_cell)
dev.off()

# -------------------------------------------------
# Create individual PDFs for each genotype
# -------------------------------------------------

if("genotype" %in% names(all_samples@meta.data)) {
  genotypes <- unique(all_samples$genotype)
  
  for(geno in genotypes) {
    filename <- paste0("UMAP_", gsub("[^[:alnum:]]", "_", geno), "_detailed.pdf")
    pdf(filename, width = 10, height = 8)
    
    geno_subset <- subset(all_samples, subset = genotype == geno)
    
    # Create a comprehensive plot
    p <- DimPlot(geno_subset,
                 group.by = "celltype",
                 cols = cluster_colors,
                 label = TRUE,
                 repel = TRUE,
                 label.size = 3) +
      ggtitle(paste("Cell Type Composition -", geno)) +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
            legend.position = "right")
    
    print(p)
    
    # Add cell count statistics
    cell_counts <- table(geno_subset$celltype)
    cell_percent <- round(prop.table(cell_counts) * 100, 1)
    
    # Create a bar plot of cell type proportions
    count_data <- data.frame(
      CellType = names(cell_counts),
      Count = as.numeric(cell_counts),
      Percent = as.numeric(cell_percent)
    )
    
    p_bar <- ggplot(count_data, aes(x = reorder(CellType, -Count), y = Count, fill = CellType)) +
      geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors) +
      geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, "\n(", Percent, "%)")), 
                vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
      labs(title = paste("Cell Type Distribution -", geno),
           x = "Cell Type", y = "Number of Cells") +
      theme_minimal() +
      theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
            plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
    
    print(p_bar)
    dev.off()
  }
}

# -------------------------------------------------
# Summary statistics across genotypes
# -------------------------------------------------
if("genotype" %in% names(all_samples@meta.data)) {
  # Create a summary table
  summary_table <- table(all_samples$genotype, all_samples$celltype)
  summary_percent <- round(prop.table(summary_table, 1) * 100, 1)
  
  write.csv(summary_table, "celltype_counts_by_genotype.csv")
  write.csv(summary_percent, "celltype_percent_by_genotype.csv")
  
  print("Summary statistics saved to CSV files")
  print("Cell counts by genotype:")
  print(summary_table)
  print("Cell percentages by genotype:")
  print(summary_percent)
}

```

```{r spatial plots}

library(Seurat)
library(ggplot2)
library(plyr)
library(patchwork) # For combining plots

# -------------------------------------------------
# define color palette (balanced + distinct)
# -------------------------------------------------
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # Deep-layer excitatory
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # Upper-layer excitatory
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Interneurons (Lhx6+)
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / vascular
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / immature oligos
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / macrophages
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 corticothalamic PNs
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# cluster identity labels
# -------------------------------------------------
cluster_labels <- c(
  "0" = "L5/6 Excitatory PNs",
  "1" = "Striatal GABAergic (MSNs)",
  "2" = "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs",
  "3" = "Astrocytes / Radial Glia",
  "4" = "Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
  "5" = "Endothelial / Vascular",
  "6" = "OPCs / Immature Oligos",
  "7" = "Microglia / Macrophages",
  "8" = "L2/3 IT PNs",
  "9" = "L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs"
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# map cluster numbers to names
# -------------------------------------------------
all_samples$celltype <- plyr::mapvalues(
  as.character(all_samples$seurat_clusters),
  from = names(cluster_labels),
  to = cluster_labels
)

# -------------------------------------------------
# Check available genotypes
# -------------------------------------------------
print("Available genotypes:")
genotypes <- unique(all_samples$genotype)
print(genotypes)

# -------------------------------------------------
# Create spatial plots for each genotype
# -------------------------------------------------

# Method 1: Individual PDFs for each genotype
for(geno in genotypes) {
  # Create filename
  filename <- paste0("SpatialPlot_", gsub("[^[:alnum:]]", "_", geno), ".pdf")
  pdf(filename, width = 12, height = 8)
  
  # Subset by genotype
  geno_subset <- subset(all_samples, subset = genotype == geno)
  
  # Create spatial plot with cluster labels
  p <- ImageDimPlot(geno_subset,
                    group.by = "seurat_clusters",
                    cols = cluster_colors,
                    size = 0.5,
                    dark.background = FALSE) +
    ggtitle(paste("Spatial Distribution -", geno)) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "right",
          legend.text = element_text(size = 10))
  
  print(p)
  dev.off()
  
  # Also create version with cell type names
  filename2 <- paste0("SpatialPlot_CellTypes_", gsub("[^[:alnum:]]", "_", geno), ".pdf")
  pdf(filename2, width = 12, height = 8)
  
  p2 <- ImageDimPlot(geno_subset,
                     group.by = "celltype",
                     cols = cluster_colors,
                     size = 0.5,
                     dark.background = FALSE) +
    ggtitle(paste("Spatial Cell Types -", geno)) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
          legend.position = "right",
          legend.text = element_text(size = 9))
  
  print(p2)
  dev.off()
}

# -------------------------------------------------
# Method 2: Combined PDF with all genotypes
# -------------------------------------------------
pdf("SpatialPlots_AllGenotypes_Combined.pdf", width = 16, height = 12)

# Create a list to store plots
spatial_plots_numeric <- list()
spatial_plots_celltype <- list()

for(i in 1:length(genotypes)) {
  geno <- genotypes[i]
  geno_subset <- subset(all_samples, subset = genotype == geno)
  
  # Numeric clusters
  spatial_plots_numeric[[geno]] <- ImageDimPlot(geno_subset,
                                                group.by = "seurat_clusters",
                                                cols = cluster_colors,
                                                size = 0.5,
                                                dark.background = FALSE) +
    ggtitle(geno) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
          legend.position = "right")
  
  # Cell type labels
  spatial_plots_celltype[[geno]] <- ImageDimPlot(geno_subset,
                                                 group.by = "celltype",
                                                 cols = cluster_colors,
                                                 size = 0.5,
                                                 dark.background = FALSE) +
    ggtitle(geno) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
          legend.position = "right")
}

# Arrange numeric cluster plots
if(length(spatial_plots_numeric) > 0) {
  combined_numeric <- wrap_plots(spatial_plots_numeric, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_annotation(title = "Spatial Distribution by Genotype - Numeric Clusters",
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18)))
  print(combined_numeric)
}

# Arrange cell type plots
if(length(spatial_plots_celltype) > 0) {
  combined_celltype <- wrap_plots(spatial_plots_celltype, ncol = 2) + 
    plot_annotation(title = "Spatial Distribution by Genotype - Cell Types",
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18)))
  print(combined_celltype)
}

dev.off()




# Show cell counts by genotype and cluster
cat("\nCell counts by genotype and cluster:\n")
print(table(all_samples$genotype, all_samples$seurat_clusters))


```
```{r List of genes to visualize on umap}



cluster_labels <- c(
  "0" = "L5/6 Excitatory PNs",
  "1" = "Striatal GABAergic (MSNs)",
  "2" = "L2/3 Excitatory PNs",
  "3" = "Astrocytes / Radial Glia",
  "4" = "Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
  "5" = "Endothelial / Vascular",
  "6" = "OPCs / Immature Oligos",
  "7" = "Microglia / Macrophages",
  "8" = "L6b IT PNs",
  "9" = "L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs"
)

# relabel
all_samples <- RenameIdents(all_samples, cluster_labels)

all_samples$celltype_ordered <- factor(
  Idents(all_samples),
  levels = desired_cluster_order
)
table(all_samples$celltype_ordered, useNA = "ifany")














# Manual ordering for optimal diagonal pattern
manual_gene_order <- c(
  # L2/3 Excitatory PNs
  "Cux1", "Cux2", "Pou3f2",
  # L5/6 Excitatory PNs
  "Bcl11b", "Fezf2",
  # L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs
  "Nr4a2", "Neurod6", "Cbln1",
    # L6b IT PNs  
  "Cplx3", "Nxph3",
  # Cortical Interneurons
  "Gad1", "Gad2", "Lhx6",
  # Striatal GABAergic
  "Ppp1r1b", "Drd1", "Drd2",
  # Astrocytes
  "Gfap",
  # OPCs/Oligos
  "Olig1", "Pdgfra", "Sox10",
  # Endothelial
  "Cldn5", "Pecam1", "Kdr",
  # Microglia
  "Trem2", "Spp1", "Siglech"
)

# Filter to available genes
available_manual <- manual_gene_order[manual_gene_order %in% rownames(all_samples)]

# Define the desired cluster order (from top to bottom)
desired_cluster_order <- c(
  "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs",
  "L5/6 Excitatory PNs",
  "L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs",
  "L6b IT PNs", 
  "Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
  "Striatal GABAergic (MSNs)",
  "Astrocytes / Radial Glia", 
  "OPCs / Immature Oligos",
  "Endothelial / Vascular",
  "Microglia / Macrophages"
)

# Reorder the cluster identities in the Seurat object
all_samples$celltype_ordered <- factor(
  Idents(all_samples),
  levels = desired_cluster_order
)

# Create manual organized dot plot with reordered clusters
manual_dot <- DotPlot(
  all_samples,
  features = available_manual,
  cols = c("lightgrey", "blue"),
  dot.scale = 6,
  assay = "SCT",
  group.by = "celltype_ordered"  # Use the reordered factor
) + 
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10)
  ) +
  ggtitle("Organized Cell Type Markers") +
  labs(x = "Markers", y = "Cell Types")

ggsave("dotplot_manual_organization_blue.pdf", manual_dot, width = 16, height = 8)












```

```{r subset WT sample and make plots}
# Subset to only the 1xWT_929 sample
wt_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = sample == "1xWT_929")


# Define genes of interest and their corresponding clusters
gene_cluster_map <- list(
  "Pou3f2" = c("2"),           # Upper-layer excitatory
  "Fezf2" = c("0"),            # Deep-layer excitatory
  "Ppp1r1b" = c("1"),          # Striatal GABAergic
  "Gfap" = c("3"),             # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "Olig1" = c("6"),            # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pdgfra1" = c("6"),          # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pecam1" = c("5")            # Endothelial / vascular
)

# Check which genes are available
available_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(wt_sample)]
missing_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[!names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(wt_sample)]

cat("Available genes:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
  cat("Missing genes:", paste(missing_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

# Define your custom cluster colors
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # Deep-layer excitatory
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # Upper-layer excitatory
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Interneurons (Lhx6+)
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / vascular
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / immature oligos
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / macrophages
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 corticothalamic PNs
)

# Create a function to plot gene with cluster-specific coloring
plot_gene_cluster_specific <- function(seurat_obj, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors) {
  # Get cluster assignments
  clusters <- as.character(Idents(seurat_obj))
  
  # Create a new grouping: target clusters get their color, others are "Other"
  plot_groups <- ifelse(clusters %in% target_clusters, clusters, "Other")
  
  # Set factor levels to ensure "Other" comes first
  plot_groups <- factor(plot_groups, levels = c("Other", target_clusters))
  
  # Add to metadata temporarily
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- plot_groups
  
  # Create color scale - grey for "Other", cluster colors for target clusters
  plot_colors <- c("Other" = "grey", cluster_colors[target_clusters])
  
  # Create the plots
  p_umap <- DimPlot(seurat_obj,
                   group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                   cols = plot_colors) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- UMAP")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  p_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(seurat_obj,
                           group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                           cols = plot_colors,
                           size = 0.8) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- Spatial")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  # Remove temporary column
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- NULL
  
  return(list(umap = p_umap, spatial = p_spatial))
}

# Create a combined PDF with UMAP and spatial side by side for each gene
pdf("1xWT_929_combined_markers.pdf", width = 16, height = 8)

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(wt_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  print(combined_plot)
  
  cat("Plotted combined view for:", gene, "for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

dev.off()

# Also create individual combined PDFs for each gene
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(wt_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  # Save combined plot
  ggsave(paste0("1xWT_929_combined_", gene, ".pdf"), 
         combined_plot, width = 16, height = 8)
  
  cat("Saved combined plot for:", gene, "\n")
}

# Create a summary PDF with all genes in a grid
pdf("1xWT_929_all_markers_grid.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)

# Create a list to store all plots
all_plots <- list()

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(wt_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Add gene name as subtitle to each plot
  plots$umap <- plots$umap + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  plots$spatial <- plots$spatial + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_umap")]] <- plots$umap
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_spatial")]] <- plots$spatial
}

# Arrange all plots in a grid
# UMAPs in first row, spatial in second row
if (length(all_plots) > 0) {
  # Create a grid layout
  grid_plot <- wrap_plots(all_plots, ncol = length(available_genes), nrow = 2) +
    plot_annotation(title = "Marker Gene Expression - 1xWT_929",
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold")))
  
  print(grid_plot)
}

dev.off()

# Create a legend with cluster colors
cluster_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Cluster = names(cluster_colors),
  Color = cluster_colors,
  CellType = c(
    "Deep-layer excitatory",
    "Striatal GABAergic", 
    "Upper-layer excitatory",
    "Astrocytes / radial glia",
    "Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
    "Endothelial / vascular",
    "OPCs / immature oligos",
    "Microglia / macrophages",
    "L2/3 IT PNs",
    "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs"
  )
)

# Create a simple legend plot
legend_plot <- ggplot(cluster_legend_data, aes(x = Cluster, y = 1, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors, name = "Clusters") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Cluster, "-", CellType)), hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 3) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Cluster Color Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

# Save the legend
ggsave("cluster_color_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 10, height = 6)

# Create a summary table of gene-cluster relationships
gene_cluster_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = available_genes,
  Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", ")),
  Cluster_Names = sapply(available_genes, function(g) {
    clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[g]]
    names <- c()
    if ("0" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Deep-layer excitatory")
    if ("1" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Striatal GABAergic")
    if ("2" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Upper-layer excitatory")
    if ("3" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Astrocytes/radial glia")
    if ("4" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Interneurons")
    if ("5" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Endothelial/vascular")
    if ("6" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "OPCs/immature oligos")
    if ("7" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Microglia/macrophages")
    if ("8" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L2/3 IT PNs")
    if ("9" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs")
    paste(names, collapse = "; ")
  })
)

print(gene_cluster_summary)
write.csv(gene_cluster_summary, "gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("All plots completed! Check the PDF files and CSV summary.\n")
cat("Generated:\n")
cat("- 1xWT_929_combined_markers.pdf: Each gene with UMAP + spatial side by side\n")
cat("- Individual combined PDFs for each gene\n")
cat("- 1xWT_929_all_markers_grid.pdf: All genes in a grid layout\n")
cat("- cluster_color_legend.pdf: Legend showing cluster colors\n")
cat("- gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv: Summary table\n")










# Create combined plots with all markers showing simultaneously

# Create a new grouping that combines all target clusters from all genes
all_target_clusters <- unique(unlist(gene_cluster_map[available_genes]))

# Create a new metadata column that labels cells with their marker gene groups
wt_sample$marker_group <- "Other"  # Default to "Other"

# For each gene, label the cells in its target clusters
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  # Find cells in these target clusters that haven't been assigned yet
  cells_to_label <- which(Idents(wt_sample) %in% target_clusters & wt_sample$marker_group == "Other")
  if (length(cells_to_label) > 0) {
    wt_sample$marker_group[cells_to_label] <- gene
  }
}

# Create color mapping for the marker groups
marker_colors <- c()
for (gene in available_genes) {
  # Use the cluster color of the first target cluster for this gene
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  if (length(target_clusters) > 0) {
    marker_colors[gene] <- cluster_colors[target_clusters[1]]
  }
}
marker_colors["Other"] <- "grey"

# Create the combined UMAP plot
p_combined_umap <- DimPlot(wt_sample,
                          group.by = "marker_group",
                          cols = marker_colors,
                          label = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - UMAP\n1xWT_929") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Create the combined spatial plot
p_combined_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(wt_sample,
                                  group.by = "marker_group",
                                  cols = marker_colors,
                                  size = 0.8) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - Spatial\n1xWT_929") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Print the combined plots
print(p_combined_umap)
print(p_combined_spatial)

# Save the combined plots
ggsave("1xWT_929_all_markers_umap.pdf", p_combined_umap, width = 12, height = 10)
ggsave("1xWT_929_all_markers_spatial.pdf", p_combined_spatial, width = 12, height = 10)

# Also create a side-by-side comparison
p_side_by_side <- p_combined_umap + p_combined_spatial +
  plot_annotation(title = "All Marker Genes - 1xWT_929",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 18, face = "bold")))

ggsave("1xWT_929_all_markers_comparison.pdf", p_side_by_side, width = 20, height = 10)

# Create a legend for the marker genes
marker_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Marker = available_genes,
  Color = marker_colors[available_genes],
  Target_Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", "))
)

legend_plot <- ggplot(marker_legend_data, aes(x = Marker, y = 1, fill = Marker)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = marker_colors, name = "Markers") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Marker, "- Clusters:", Target_Clusters)), 
            hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 4) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Marker Gene Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

ggsave("marker_gene_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 12, height = 6)

cat("Created combined plots with all markers:\n")
cat("- 1xWT_929_all_markers_umap.pdf: UMAP with all markers\n")
cat("- 1xWT_929_all_markers_spatial.pdf: Spatial with all markers\n")
cat("- 1xWT_929_all_markers_comparison.pdf: UMAP and spatial side by side\n")
cat("- marker_gene_legend.pdf: Legend for marker genes\n")
```

```{r subset LD sample and make plots}
# Use LD_3 replicate for analysis
ld_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = section == "LD_3")

# Define genes of interest and their corresponding clusters
gene_cluster_map <- list(
  "Pou3f2" = c("2"),           # Upper-layer excitatory
  "Fezf2" = c("0"),            # Deep-layer excitatory
  "Ppp1r1b" = c("1"),          # Striatal GABAergic
  "Gfap" = c("3"),             # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "Olig1" = c("6"),            # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pdgfra1" = c("6"),          # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pecam1" = c("5")            # Endothelial / vascular
)

# Check which genes are available
available_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(ld_sample)]
missing_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[!names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(ld_sample)]

cat("Available genes:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
  cat("Missing genes:", paste(missing_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

# Define your custom cluster colors
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # Deep-layer excitatory
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # Upper-layer excitatory
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Interneurons (Lhx6+)
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / vascular
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / immature oligos
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / macrophages
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 corticothalamic PNs
)

# Create a function to plot gene with cluster-specific coloring
plot_gene_cluster_specific <- function(seurat_obj, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors) {
  # Get cluster assignments
  clusters <- as.character(Idents(seurat_obj))
  
  # Create a new grouping: target clusters get their color, others are "Other"
  plot_groups <- ifelse(clusters %in% target_clusters, clusters, "Other")
  
  # Set factor levels to ensure "Other" comes first
  plot_groups <- factor(plot_groups, levels = c("Other", target_clusters))
  
  # Add to metadata temporarily
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- plot_groups
  
  # Create color scale - grey for "Other", cluster colors for target clusters
  plot_colors <- c("Other" = "grey", cluster_colors[target_clusters])
  
  # Create the plots
  p_umap <- DimPlot(seurat_obj,
                   group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                   cols = plot_colors) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- UMAP")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  p_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(seurat_obj,
                           group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                           cols = plot_colors,
                           size = 0.8) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- Spatial")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  # Remove temporary column
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- NULL
  
  return(list(umap = p_umap, spatial = p_spatial))
}

# Create a combined PDF with UMAP and spatial side by side for each gene
pdf("LD_3_combined_markers.pdf", width = 16, height = 8)

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(ld_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  print(combined_plot)
  
  cat("Plotted combined view for:", gene, "for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

dev.off()

# Also create individual combined PDFs for each gene
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(ld_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  # Save combined plot
  ggsave(paste0("LD_3_combined_", gene, ".pdf"), 
         combined_plot, width = 16, height = 8)
  
  cat("Saved combined plot for:", gene, "\n")
}

# Create a summary PDF with all genes in a grid
pdf("LD_3_all_markers_grid.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)

# Create a list to store all plots
all_plots <- list()

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(ld_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Add gene name as subtitle to each plot
  plots$umap <- plots$umap + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  plots$spatial <- plots$spatial + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_umap")]] <- plots$umap
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_spatial")]] <- plots$spatial
}

# Arrange all plots in a grid
# UMAPs in first row, spatial in second row
if (length(all_plots) > 0) {
  # Create a grid layout
  grid_plot <- wrap_plots(all_plots, ncol = length(available_genes), nrow = 2) +
    plot_annotation(title = "Marker Gene Expression - LD_3",
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold")))
  
  print(grid_plot)
}

dev.off()

# Create a legend with cluster colors
cluster_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Cluster = names(cluster_colors),
  Color = cluster_colors,
  CellType = c(
    "Deep-layer excitatory",
    "Striatal GABAergic", 
    "Upper-layer excitatory",
    "Astrocytes / radial glia",
    "Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
    "Endothelial / vascular",
    "OPCs / immature oligos",
    "Microglia / macrophages",
    "L2/3 IT PNs",
    "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs"
  )
)

# Create a simple legend plot
legend_plot <- ggplot(cluster_legend_data, aes(x = Cluster, y = 1, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors, name = "Clusters") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Cluster, "-", CellType)), hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 3) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Cluster Color Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

# Save the legend
ggsave("LD_3_cluster_color_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 10, height = 6)

# Create a summary table of gene-cluster relationships
gene_cluster_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = available_genes,
  Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", ")),
  Cluster_Names = sapply(available_genes, function(g) {
    clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[g]]
    names <- c()
    if ("0" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Deep-layer excitatory")
    if ("1" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Striatal GABAergic")
    if ("2" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Upper-layer excitatory")
    if ("3" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Astrocytes/radial glia")
    if ("4" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Interneurons")
    if ("5" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Endothelial/vascular")
    if ("6" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "OPCs/immature oligos")
    if ("7" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Microglia/macrophages")
    if ("8" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L2/3 IT PNs")
    if ("9" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs")
    paste(names, collapse = "; ")
  })
)

print(gene_cluster_summary)
write.csv(gene_cluster_summary, "LD_3_gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("All plots completed! Check the PDF files and CSV summary.\n")
cat("Generated:\n")
cat("- LD_3_combined_markers.pdf: Each gene with UMAP + spatial side by side\n")
cat("- Individual combined PDFs for each gene\n")
cat("- LD_3_all_markers_grid.pdf: All genes in a grid layout\n")
cat("- LD_3_cluster_color_legend.pdf: Legend showing cluster colors\n")
cat("- LD_3_gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv: Summary table\n")

# Create combined plots with all markers showing simultaneously

# Create a new grouping that combines all target clusters from all genes
all_target_clusters <- unique(unlist(gene_cluster_map[available_genes]))

# Create a new metadata column that labels cells with their marker gene groups
ld_sample$marker_group <- "Other"  # Default to "Other"

# For each gene, label the cells in its target clusters
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  # Find cells in these target clusters that haven't been assigned yet
  cells_to_label <- which(Idents(ld_sample) %in% target_clusters & ld_sample$marker_group == "Other")
  if (length(cells_to_label) > 0) {
    ld_sample$marker_group[cells_to_label] <- gene
  }
}

# Create color mapping for the marker groups
marker_colors <- c()
for (gene in available_genes) {
  # Use the cluster color of the first target cluster for this gene
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  if (length(target_clusters) > 0) {
    marker_colors[gene] <- cluster_colors[target_clusters[1]]
  }
}
marker_colors["Other"] <- "grey"

# Create the combined UMAP plot
p_combined_umap <- DimPlot(ld_sample,
                          group.by = "marker_group",
                          cols = marker_colors,
                          label = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - UMAP\nLD_3") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Create the combined spatial plot
p_combined_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(ld_sample,
                                  group.by = "marker_group",
                                  cols = marker_colors,
                                  size = 0.8) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - Spatial\nLD_3") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Print the combined plots
print(p_combined_umap)
print(p_combined_spatial)

# Save the combined plots
ggsave("LD_3_all_markers_umap.pdf", p_combined_umap, width = 12, height = 10)
ggsave("LD_3_all_markers_spatial.pdf", p_combined_spatial, width = 12, height = 10)

# Also create a side-by-side comparison
p_side_by_side <- p_combined_umap + p_combined_spatial +
  plot_annotation(title = "All Marker Genes - LD_3",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 18, face = "bold")))

ggsave("LD_3_all_markers_comparison.pdf", p_side_by_side, width = 20, height = 10)

# Create a legend for the marker genes
marker_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Marker = available_genes,
  Color = marker_colors[available_genes],
  Target_Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", "))
)

legend_plot <- ggplot(marker_legend_data, aes(x = Marker, y = 1, fill = Marker)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = marker_colors, name = "Markers") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Marker, "- Clusters:", Target_Clusters)), 
            hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 4) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Marker Gene Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

ggsave("LD_3_marker_gene_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 12, height = 6)

cat("Created combined plots with all markers:\n")
cat("- LD_3_all_markers_umap.pdf: UMAP with all markers\n")
cat("- LD_3_all_markers_spatial.pdf: Spatial with all markers\n")
cat("- LD_3_all_markers_comparison.pdf: UMAP and spatial side by side\n")
cat("- LD_3_marker_gene_legend.pdf: Legend for marker genes\n")
```


```{r Subset LD_NAC and make plots}
# Use LD_NAC_5 replicate for analysis
ld_nac_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = section == "LD_NAC_5")

# Define genes of interest and their corresponding clusters
gene_cluster_map <- list(
  "Pou3f2" = c("2"),           # Upper-layer excitatory
  "Fezf2" = c("0"),            # Deep-layer excitatory
  "Ppp1r1b" = c("1"),          # Striatal GABAergic
  "Gfap" = c("3"),             # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "Olig1" = c("6"),            # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pdgfra1" = c("6"),          # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pecam1" = c("5")            # Endothelial / vascular
)

# Check which genes are available
available_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(ld_nac_sample)]
missing_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[!names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(ld_nac_sample)]

cat("Available genes:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
  cat("Missing genes:", paste(missing_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

# Define your custom cluster colors
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # Deep-layer excitatory
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # Upper-layer excitatory
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Interneurons (Lhx6+)
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / vascular
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / immature oligos
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / macrophages
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 corticothalamic PNs
)

# Create a function to plot gene with cluster-specific coloring
plot_gene_cluster_specific <- function(seurat_obj, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors) {
  # Get cluster assignments
  clusters <- as.character(Idents(seurat_obj))
  
  # Create a new grouping: target clusters get their color, others are "Other"
  plot_groups <- ifelse(clusters %in% target_clusters, clusters, "Other")
  
  # Set factor levels to ensure "Other" comes first
  plot_groups <- factor(plot_groups, levels = c("Other", target_clusters))
  
  # Add to metadata temporarily
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- plot_groups
  
  # Create color scale - grey for "Other", cluster colors for target clusters
  plot_colors <- c("Other" = "grey", cluster_colors[target_clusters])
  
  # Create the plots
  p_umap <- DimPlot(seurat_obj,
                   group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                   cols = plot_colors) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- UMAP")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  p_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(seurat_obj,
                           group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                           cols = plot_colors,
                           size = 0.8) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- Spatial")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  # Remove temporary column
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- NULL
  
  return(list(umap = p_umap, spatial = p_spatial))
}

# Create a combined PDF with UMAP and spatial side by side for each gene
pdf("LD_NAC_5_combined_markers.pdf", width = 16, height = 8)

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(ld_nac_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  print(combined_plot)
  
  cat("Plotted combined view for:", gene, "for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

dev.off()

# Also create individual combined PDFs for each gene
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(ld_nac_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  # Save combined plot
  ggsave(paste0("LD_NAC_5_combined_", gene, ".pdf"), 
         combined_plot, width = 16, height = 8)
  
  cat("Saved combined plot for:", gene, "\n")
}

# Create a summary PDF with all genes in a grid
pdf("LD_NAC_5_all_markers_grid.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)

# Create a list to store all plots
all_plots <- list()

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(ld_nac_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Add gene name as subtitle to each plot
  plots$umap <- plots$umap + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  plots$spatial <- plots$spatial + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_umap")]] <- plots$umap
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_spatial")]] <- plots$spatial
}

# Arrange all plots in a grid
# UMAPs in first row, spatial in second row
if (length(all_plots) > 0) {
  # Create a grid layout
  grid_plot <- wrap_plots(all_plots, ncol = length(available_genes), nrow = 2) +
    plot_annotation(title = "Marker Gene Expression - LD_NAC_5",
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold")))
  
  print(grid_plot)
}

dev.off()

# Create a legend with cluster colors
cluster_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Cluster = names(cluster_colors),
  Color = cluster_colors,
  CellType = c(
    "Deep-layer excitatory",
    "Striatal GABAergic", 
    "Upper-layer excitatory",
    "Astrocytes / radial glia",
    "Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
    "Endothelial / vascular",
    "OPCs / immature oligos",
    "Microglia / macrophages",
    "L2/3 IT PNs",
    "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs"
  )
)

# Create a simple legend plot
legend_plot <- ggplot(cluster_legend_data, aes(x = Cluster, y = 1, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors, name = "Clusters") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Cluster, "-", CellType)), hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 3) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Cluster Color Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

# Save the legend
ggsave("LD_NAC_5_cluster_color_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 10, height = 6)

# Create a summary table of gene-cluster relationships
gene_cluster_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = available_genes,
  Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", ")),
  Cluster_Names = sapply(available_genes, function(g) {
    clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[g]]
    names <- c()
    if ("0" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Deep-layer excitatory")
    if ("1" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Striatal GABAergic")
    if ("2" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Upper-layer excitatory")
    if ("3" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Astrocytes/radial glia")
    if ("4" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Interneurons")
    if ("5" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Endothelial/vascular")
    if ("6" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "OPCs/immature oligos")
    if ("7" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Microglia/macrophages")
    if ("8" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L2/3 IT PNs")
    if ("9" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs")
    paste(names, collapse = "; ")
  })
)

print(gene_cluster_summary)
write.csv(gene_cluster_summary, "LD_NAC_5_gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("All plots completed! Check the PDF files and CSV summary.\n")
cat("Generated:\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_combined_markers.pdf: Each gene with UMAP + spatial side by side\n")
cat("- Individual combined PDFs for each gene\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_all_markers_grid.pdf: All genes in a grid layout\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_cluster_color_legend.pdf: Legend showing cluster colors\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv: Summary table\n")

# Create combined plots with all markers showing simultaneously

# Create a new grouping that combines all target clusters from all genes
all_target_clusters <- unique(unlist(gene_cluster_map[available_genes]))

# Create a new metadata column that labels cells with their marker gene groups
ld_nac_sample$marker_group <- "Other"  # Default to "Other"

# For each gene, label the cells in its target clusters
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  # Find cells in these target clusters that haven't been assigned yet
  cells_to_label <- which(Idents(ld_nac_sample) %in% target_clusters & ld_nac_sample$marker_group == "Other")
  if (length(cells_to_label) > 0) {
    ld_nac_sample$marker_group[cells_to_label] <- gene
  }
}

# Create color mapping for the marker groups
marker_colors <- c()
for (gene in available_genes) {
  # Use the cluster color of the first target cluster for this gene
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  if (length(target_clusters) > 0) {
    marker_colors[gene] <- cluster_colors[target_clusters[1]]
  }
}
marker_colors["Other"] <- "grey"

# Create the combined UMAP plot
p_combined_umap <- DimPlot(ld_nac_sample,
                          group.by = "marker_group",
                          cols = marker_colors,
                          label = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - UMAP\nLD_NAC_5") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Create the combined spatial plot
p_combined_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(ld_nac_sample,
                                  group.by = "marker_group",
                                  cols = marker_colors,
                                  size = 0.8) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - Spatial\nLD_NAC_5") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Print the combined plots
print(p_combined_umap)
print(p_combined_spatial)

# Save the combined plots
ggsave("LD_NAC_5_all_markers_umap.pdf", p_combined_umap, width = 12, height = 10)
ggsave("LD_NAC_5_all_markers_spatial.pdf", p_combined_spatial, width = 12, height = 10)

# Also create a side-by-side comparison
p_side_by_side <- p_combined_umap + p_combined_spatial +
  plot_annotation(title = "All Marker Genes - LD_NAC_5",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 18, face = "bold")))

ggsave("LD_NAC_5_all_markers_comparison.pdf", p_side_by_side, width = 20, height = 10)

# Create a legend for the marker genes
marker_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Marker = available_genes,
  Color = marker_colors[available_genes],
  Target_Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", "))
)

legend_plot <- ggplot(marker_legend_data, aes(x = Marker, y = 1, fill = Marker)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = marker_colors, name = "Markers") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Marker, "- Clusters:", Target_Clusters)), 
            hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 4) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Marker Gene Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

ggsave("LD_NAC_5_marker_gene_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 12, height = 6)

cat("Created combined plots with all markers:\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_all_markers_umap.pdf: UMAP with all markers\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_all_markers_spatial.pdf: Spatial with all markers\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_all_markers_comparison.pdf: UMAP and spatial side by side\n")
cat("- LD_NAC_5_marker_gene_legend.pdf: Legend for marker genes\n")

```
```{r Subset WT_NAC and make plots}
# Use WT_NAC_1 replicate for analysis
wt_nac_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = section == "WT_NAC_1")

# Define genes of interest and their corresponding clusters
gene_cluster_map <- list(
  "Pou3f2" = c("2"),           # Upper-layer excitatory
  "Fezf2" = c("0"),            # Deep-layer excitatory
  "Ppp1r1b" = c("1"),          # Striatal GABAergic
  "Gfap" = c("3"),             # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "Olig1" = c("6"),            # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pdgfra1" = c("6"),          # OPCs / immature oligos
  "Pecam1" = c("5")            # Endothelial / vascular
)

# Check which genes are available
available_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(wt_nac_sample)]
missing_genes <- names(gene_cluster_map)[!names(gene_cluster_map) %in% rownames(wt_nac_sample)]

cat("Available genes:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
  cat("Missing genes:", paste(missing_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

# Define your custom cluster colors
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # Deep-layer excitatory
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # Upper-layer excitatory
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / radial glia
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Interneurons (Lhx6+)
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / vascular
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / immature oligos
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / macrophages
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 corticothalamic PNs
)

# Create a function to plot gene with cluster-specific coloring
plot_gene_cluster_specific <- function(seurat_obj, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors) {
  # Get cluster assignments
  clusters <- as.character(Idents(seurat_obj))
  
  # Create a new grouping: target clusters get their color, others are "Other"
  plot_groups <- ifelse(clusters %in% target_clusters, clusters, "Other")
  
  # Set factor levels to ensure "Other" comes first
  plot_groups <- factor(plot_groups, levels = c("Other", target_clusters))
  
  # Add to metadata temporarily
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- plot_groups
  
  # Create color scale - grey for "Other", cluster colors for target clusters
  plot_colors <- c("Other" = "grey", cluster_colors[target_clusters])
  
  # Create the plots
  p_umap <- DimPlot(seurat_obj,
                   group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                   cols = plot_colors) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- UMAP")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  p_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(seurat_obj,
                           group.by = "temp_plot_group",
                           cols = plot_colors,
                           size = 0.8) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- Spatial")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12, face = "bold"))
  
  # Remove temporary column
  seurat_obj$temp_plot_group <- NULL
  
  return(list(umap = p_umap, spatial = p_spatial))
}

# Create a combined PDF with UMAP and spatial side by side for each gene
pdf("WT_NAC_1_combined_markers.pdf", width = 16, height = 8)

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(wt_nac_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  print(combined_plot)
  
  cat("Plotted combined view for:", gene, "for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

dev.off()

# Also create individual combined PDFs for each gene
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(wt_nac_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Combine UMAP and spatial plots side by side
  combined_plot <- plots$umap + plots$spatial + 
    plot_annotation(title = paste(gene, "- Marker for clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")),
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")))
  
  # Save combined plot
  ggsave(paste0("WT_NAC_1_combined_", gene, ".pdf"), 
         combined_plot, width = 16, height = 8)
  
  cat("Saved combined plot for:", gene, "\n")
}

# Create a summary PDF with all genes in a grid
pdf("WT_NAC_1_all_markers_grid.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)

# Create a list to store all plots
all_plots <- list()

for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  
  plots <- plot_gene_cluster_specific(wt_nac_sample, gene, target_clusters, cluster_colors)
  
  # Add gene name as subtitle to each plot
  plots$umap <- plots$umap + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  plots$spatial <- plots$spatial + labs(subtitle = paste("Clusters:", paste(target_clusters, collapse = ", ")))
  
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_umap")]] <- plots$umap
  all_plots[[paste0(gene, "_spatial")]] <- plots$spatial
}

# Arrange all plots in a grid
# UMAPs in first row, spatial in second row
if (length(all_plots) > 0) {
  # Create a grid layout
  grid_plot <- wrap_plots(all_plots, ncol = length(available_genes), nrow = 2) +
    plot_annotation(title = "Marker Gene Expression - WT_NAC_1",
                    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold")))
  
  print(grid_plot)
}

dev.off()

# Create a legend with cluster colors
cluster_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Cluster = names(cluster_colors),
  Color = cluster_colors,
  CellType = c(
    "Deep-layer excitatory",
    "Striatal GABAergic", 
    "Upper-layer excitatory",
    "Astrocytes / radial glia",
    "Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
    "Endothelial / vascular",
    "OPCs / immature oligos",
    "Microglia / macrophages",
    "L2/3 IT PNs",
    "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs"
  )
)

# Create a simple legend plot
legend_plot <- ggplot(cluster_legend_data, aes(x = Cluster, y = 1, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors, name = "Clusters") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Cluster, "-", CellType)), hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 3) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Cluster Color Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

# Save the legend
ggsave("WT_NAC_1_cluster_color_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 10, height = 6)

# Create a summary table of gene-cluster relationships
gene_cluster_summary <- data.frame(
  Gene = available_genes,
  Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", ")),
  Cluster_Names = sapply(available_genes, function(g) {
    clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[g]]
    names <- c()
    if ("0" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Deep-layer excitatory")
    if ("1" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Striatal GABAergic")
    if ("2" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Upper-layer excitatory")
    if ("3" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Astrocytes/radial glia")
    if ("4" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Interneurons")
    if ("5" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Endothelial/vascular")
    if ("6" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "OPCs/immature oligos")
    if ("7" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "Microglia/macrophages")
    if ("8" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L2/3 IT PNs")
    if ("9" %in% clusters) names <- c(names, "L5/6 corticothalamic PNs")
    paste(names, collapse = "; ")
  })
)

print(gene_cluster_summary)
write.csv(gene_cluster_summary, "WT_NAC_1_gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("All plots completed! Check the PDF files and CSV summary.\n")
cat("Generated:\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_combined_markers.pdf: Each gene with UMAP + spatial side by side\n")
cat("- Individual combined PDFs for each gene\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_all_markers_grid.pdf: All genes in a grid layout\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_cluster_color_legend.pdf: Legend showing cluster colors\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_gene_cluster_mapping_summary.csv: Summary table\n")

# Create combined plots with all markers showing simultaneously

# Create a new grouping that combines all target clusters from all genes
all_target_clusters <- unique(unlist(gene_cluster_map[available_genes]))

# Create a new metadata column that labels cells with their marker gene groups
wt_nac_sample$marker_group <- "Other"  # Default to "Other"

# For each gene, label the cells in its target clusters
for (gene in available_genes) {
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  # Find cells in these target clusters that haven't been assigned yet
  cells_to_label <- which(Idents(wt_nac_sample) %in% target_clusters & wt_nac_sample$marker_group == "Other")
  if (length(cells_to_label) > 0) {
    wt_nac_sample$marker_group[cells_to_label] <- gene
  }
}

# Create color mapping for the marker groups
marker_colors <- c()
for (gene in available_genes) {
  # Use the cluster color of the first target cluster for this gene
  target_clusters <- gene_cluster_map[[gene]]
  if (length(target_clusters) > 0) {
    marker_colors[gene] <- cluster_colors[target_clusters[1]]
  }
}
marker_colors["Other"] <- "grey"

# Create the combined UMAP plot
p_combined_umap <- DimPlot(wt_nac_sample,
                          group.by = "marker_group",
                          cols = marker_colors,
                          label = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - UMAP\nWT_NAC_1") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Create the combined spatial plot
p_combined_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(wt_nac_sample,
                                  group.by = "marker_group",
                                  cols = marker_colors,
                                  size = 0.8) +
  ggtitle("Combined Marker Expression - Spatial\nWT_NAC_1") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold")) +
  labs(subtitle = paste("Markers:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", ")))

# Print the combined plots
print(p_combined_umap)
print(p_combined_spatial)

# Save the combined plots
ggsave("WT_NAC_1_all_markers_umap.pdf", p_combined_umap, width = 12, height = 10)
ggsave("WT_NAC_1_all_markers_spatial.pdf", p_combined_spatial, width = 12, height = 10)

# Also create a side-by-side comparison
p_side_by_side <- p_combined_umap + p_combined_spatial +
  plot_annotation(title = "All Marker Genes - WT_NAC_1",
                  theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 18, face = "bold")))

ggsave("WT_NAC_1_all_markers_comparison.pdf", p_side_by_side, width = 20, height = 10)

# Create a legend for the marker genes
marker_legend_data <- data.frame(
  Marker = available_genes,
  Color = marker_colors[available_genes],
  Target_Clusters = sapply(available_genes, function(g) paste(gene_cluster_map[[g]], collapse = ", "))
)

legend_plot <- ggplot(marker_legend_data, aes(x = Marker, y = 1, fill = Marker)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = marker_colors, name = "Markers") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste(Marker, "- Clusters:", Target_Clusters)), 
            hjust = 0, nudge_y = 0.5, size = 4) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.1) +
  ggtitle("Marker Gene Legend") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))

ggsave("WT_NAC_1_marker_gene_legend.pdf", legend_plot, width = 12, height = 6)

cat("Created combined plots with all markers:\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_all_markers_umap.pdf: UMAP with all markers\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_all_markers_spatial.pdf: Spatial with all markers\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_all_markers_comparison.pdf: UMAP and spatial side by side\n")
cat("- WT_NAC_1_marker_gene_legend.pdf: Legend for marker genes\n")



```
```{r 22q11 all samples plots}


# Comprehensive visualization of 22q11 gene expression
## 22q11 genes list
genes_22q11 <- c(
  "Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt", "Tango2", "Mrpl40", 
  "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8", "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh"
)

# Filter to available genes
available_22q11 <- genes_22q11[genes_22q11 %in% rownames(all_samples)]

# 1. Violin plots for each gene
cat("Creating violin plots...\n")
vln_plots <- VlnPlot(all_samples,
                    features = available_22q11,
                    group.by = "genotype",
                    ncol = 4,
                    pt.size = 0,
                    raster = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("22q11 Genes Expression - all samples") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

print(vln_plots)
ggsave("all_samples_22q11_genes_violin_plots.pdf", vln_plots, width = 16, height = 12)

# 2. Dot plot (shows expression level and percentage)
cat("Creating dot plot...\n")
dot_plot <- DotPlot(all_samples,
                   features = available_22q11,
                   group.by = "genotype",
                   cols = c("lightblue", "red"),
                   dot.scale = 6) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic")) +
  ggtitle("22q11 Genes - Expression Level and Percentage") +
  labs(x = "22q11 Genes", y = "Genotype")

print(dot_plot)
ggsave("allsamples_22q11_genes_dot_plot.pdf", dot_plot, width = 12, height = 8)




# Alternative direct approach
cat("Creating heatmap using direct calculation...\n")

# Get expression data and metadata
expr_data <- FetchData(all_samples, vars = c(available_22q11, "genotype"))

# Calculate mean expression per genotype
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

avg_exp_long <- expr_data %>%
  group_by(genotype) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(available_22q11), mean, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -genotype, names_to = "Gene", values_to = "Expression")

# Apply desired genotype order
desired_genotype_order <- c("WT-NAC", "LgDel_NAC", "LgDel", "WT")
avg_exp_long$genotype <- factor(avg_exp_long$genotype, levels = desired_genotype_order)

# Remove any NA genotypes
avg_exp_long <- avg_exp_long[!is.na(avg_exp_long$genotype), ]

# Create heatmap
heatmap_plot <- ggplot(avg_exp_long, aes(x = genotype, y = Gene, fill = Expression)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", 
                      midpoint = mean(avg_exp_long$Expression, na.rm = TRUE),
                      name = "Expression\nLevel") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic", size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14)
  ) +
  labs(title = "22q11 Genes - Average Expression by Genotype",
       x = "Genotype", y = "Gene") +
  coord_fixed()

print(heatmap_plot)
ggsave("all_samples_22q11_genes_heatmap_direct.pdf", heatmap_plot, width = 10, height = 8)

# Create dot plot with custom genotype order and no grid lines
cat("Creating ordered dot plot...\n")

# First, let's check the current order and reorder if needed
print("Current genotype order:")
print(levels(all_samples@meta.data$genotype))

# Reorder the genotype factor if needed
all_samples@meta.data$genotype <- factor(all_samples@meta.data$genotype, 
                                        levels = c("WT", "LD", "LD_NAC", "WT_NAC"))

dot_plot <- DotPlot(all_samples,
                   features = available_22q11,
                   group.by = "genotype",
                   cols = c("lightblue", "red"),
                   dot.scale = 8,
                   dot.min = 0.1) +
  theme_classic() +  # theme_classic has no grid by default
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10, face = "italic"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(size = 0.5, color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    # Ensure no grid lines (redundant with theme_classic but safe)
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    # Adjust spacing
    plot.margin = unit(c(0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5), "cm")
  ) +
  ggtitle("22q11 Genes Expression") +
  labs(color = "Average\nExpression", size = "Percent\nExpressed")

print(dot_plot)
ggsave("22q11_genes_dot_plot_ordered.pdf", dot_plot, width = 10, height = 6)


```

```{r flipped 22q11 dotplot}
cat("Creating flipped dot plot...\n")

# Extract data from DotPlot
dp_data <- DotPlot(all_samples,
                   features = available_22q11,
                   group.by = "genotype",
                   dot.scale = 6)$data

# Flip the mappings: now color = pct.exp, size = avg.exp.scaled
flipped_dot <- ggplot(dp_data, aes(x = features.plot, y = id)) +
```
