```{r flipped 22q11 dotplot}
  geom_point(aes(size = avg.exp.scaled, color = pct.exp)) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "lightblue", high = "red") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 8)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
        panel.grid = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("22q11 Genes - Flipped Aesthetics") +
  labs(x = "22q11 Genes", y = "Genotype",
       color = "% Expressed", size = "Average Expression")

print(flipped_dot)

ggsave("allsamples_22q11_genes_dot_plot_flipped.pdf", flipped_dot, width = 12, height = 8)









cat("Creating heatmap...\n")

library(ggplot2)

# Extract the same data used by DotPlot
dp_data <- DotPlot(all_samples,
                   features = available_22q11,
                   group.by = "genotype")$data

# Plot as heatmap: x = gene, y = genotype, fill = avg.exp.scaled
heatmap_plot <- ggplot(dp_data, aes(x = features.plot, y = id, fill = avg.exp.scaled)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("lightblue", "white", "red")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right") +
  labs(x = "22q11 Genes", y = "Genotype", fill = "Avg. Expression (scaled)") +
  ggtitle("22q11 Gene Expression Across Genotypes")

print(heatmap_plot)

ggsave("allsamples_22q11_genes_heatmap.pdf", heatmap_plot, width = 10, height = 6)
```


```{r test heatmap by aggregate}
cat("Creating heatmap using aggregate expression...\n")

library(Seurat)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

aggregate_expr <- AverageExpression(
  all_samples,
  features = available_22q11,
  group.by = "genotype",
  assays = "Xenium",
  slot = "counts"
)

str(aggregate_expr)
library(Matrix)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

cat("Creating heatmap using aggregate expression...\n")

# Extract the matrix from the list
aggregate_expr <- aggregate_expr$Xenium

# Convert sparse matrix to a data frame
agg_df <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(aggregate_expr))

# Add gene names as a column
agg_df$Gene <- rownames(agg_df)

# Convert to long format
agg_long <- pivot_longer(
  agg_df,
  cols = c("LD", "LD-NAC", "WT", "WT-NAC"),
  names_to = "Genotype",
  values_to = "Aggregate_Expression"
)

# Log-transform for visualization
agg_long$logExpr <- log1p(agg_long$Aggregate_Expression)

# Plot the heatmap
heatmap_plot <- ggplot(agg_long, aes(x = Gene, y = Genotype, fill = logExpr)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colors = c("lightblue", "white", "red")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right") +
  labs(x = "22q11 Genes", y = "Genotype", fill = "log(1+Aggregate Expression)") +
  ggtitle("22q11 Genes - Aggregate Expression Across Genotypes")

print(heatmap_plot)

ggsave("allsamples_22q11_genes_heatmap_aggregate.pdf", heatmap_plot, width = 10, height = 6)



```

```{r 22q11 heatmap for all_samples}
# Load required package
library(circlize)
library(ComplexHeatmap)

# Create one PDF with 22q11 genes heatmap (averaged by genotype)
pdf("22q11_genes_heatmap_all_samples.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)

# 22q11 Genes only
gene_sets <- list(
  "22q11 Genes" = c("Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt", "Tango2", "Mrpl40", 
                    "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8", "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh")
)

# Create heatmap for 22q11 genes
for(i in 1:length(gene_sets)) {
  set_name <- names(gene_sets)[i]
  gene_list <- gene_sets[[i]]
  
  # Get genes that are actually in the dataset
  genes_found <- gene_list[gene_list %in% rownames(all_samples)]
  
  if(length(genes_found) > 0) {
    # Calculate average expression by genotype
    avg_expression <- AverageExpression(
      all_samples,
      features = genes_found,
      group.by = "genotype",
      assays = "SCT",
      slot = "data"
    )$SCT
    
    # DEBUG: Check what genotypes we actually have
    cat("Available genotypes in data:", paste(colnames(avg_expression), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    
    # Define genotype order based on what's actually in the data
    # Use whatever order AverageExpression returns, or specify based on what we see
    genotype_order <- colnames(avg_expression)  # Use natural order
    
    # If you want a specific order and the genotypes match, you can reorder:
    # genotype_order <- c("WT", "WT_NAC", "LD", "LD_NAC")
    # But only use this if all these genotypes exist in colnames(avg_expression)
    
    # Z-score normalization (row-wise)
    mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(avg_expression)))
    
    # Create color scale
    col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("navy", "white", "firebrick"))
    
    # Create heatmap
    hm <- Heatmap(
      mat_scaled,
      name = "Z-score",
      cluster_rows = TRUE,
      cluster_columns = FALSE,  # Use the natural order from the data
      col = col_fun,
      row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10, fontface = "italic"),
      column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 12, fontface = "bold"),
      heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Expression (z-score)"),
      column_title = paste(set_name, "(", length(genes_found), "genes )"),
      row_title = NULL,
      show_row_names = TRUE,
      show_column_names = TRUE,
      rect_gp = gpar(col = "white", lwd = 1)  # Add white grid lines
    )
    
    draw(hm)
    cat("Plotted", set_name, "with", length(genes_found), "genes\n")
  } else {
    cat("No genes found for:", set_name, "\n")
  }
}

dev.off()
cat("Saved 22q11 genes heatmap: 22q11_genes_heatmap_all_samples.pdf\n")


```
```{r all_samples cell proportion analysis across all clusters - ANOVA with Dunnett Post-hoc}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(multcomp)
library(purrr)

# 0) choose object + cluster column (works for all_samples or l23)
obj <- all_samples                    # or l23
cluster_col <- "seurat_clusters"      # or your subcluster column

# 1) per-animal proportions per cluster
prop_per_animal <- obj@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(genotype, animal_id, !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(cluster = !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 2) mean±SE per genotype (for plotting/reporting)
grp_summary <- prop_per_animal %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_prop = mean(prop),
    se = sd(prop) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
    n_animals = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3) ANOVA + Dunnett (WT as reference) per cluster
run_one <- function(df, ref = "WT") {
  df$genotype <- stats::relevel(factor(df$genotype), ref = ref)
  fit <- aov(prop ~ genotype, data = df)
  anova_p <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

  dun <- multcomp::glht(fit, linfct = mcp(genotype = "Dunnett"))
  ds <- summary(dun)

  tibble::tibble(
    contrast = names(ds$test$coefficients),
    estimate = as.numeric(ds$test$coefficients),   # difference vs ref (WT)
    t        = as.numeric(ds$test$tstat),
    p_adj    = as.numeric(ds$test$pvalues),
    anova_p  = anova_p
  )
}

all_clusters <- sort(unique(prop_per_animal$cluster))

dunnett_by_cluster <- purrr::map_dfr(
  all_clusters,
  ~ run_one(dplyr::filter(prop_per_animal, cluster == .x), ref = "WT") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(cluster = .x),
  .id = NULL
) %>%
  dplyr::relocate(cluster, .before = contrast) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(contrast) %>%                      # BH adjust across clusters per contrast
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj_BH_across_clusters = p.adjust(p_adj, method = "BH")) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

## ----- 4) Quick visuals + Save as PDF -----

# (a) Heatmap of mean proportions (%)
heat_df <- grp_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(pct = 100 * mean_prop)

pdf(file.path(save.location, "all_samples__Cluster_Proportion_Heatmap.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = factor(cluster), y = genotype, fill = pct)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Genotype",
    fill = "% Cells",
    title = "all_samples_Proportion per Cluster (Mean Across Animals)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
dev.off()


# (b) Faceted bars with mean ± SE
pdf(file.path(save.location, "all_samples_Cluster_Proportion_FacetedBars.pdf"), width = 10, height = 7)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_prop)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_prop - se, ymax = mean_prop + se), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proportion of Cells",
    title = "all_samples_Cluster Proportions by Genotype (Mean ± SE)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
dev.off()


# (c) Stacked column plot: cluster composition by genotype
grp_summary$cluster <- factor(grp_summary$cluster, levels = sort(unique(grp_summary$cluster)))
pal <- Seurat::DiscretePalette(length(levels(grp_summary$cluster)), palette = "polychrome")
names(pal) <- levels(grp_summary$cluster)

pdf(file.path(save.location, "all_samples_Cluster_Composition_StackedBars.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_prop, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75, position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = "Cluster") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "all_samples_Cluster Composition by Genotype (Mean Across Animals)",
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Composition (%)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
dev.off()

top_hits <- dunnett_by_cluster %>%
  filter(p_adj_BH_across_clusters < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(p_adj_BH_across_clusters)

ggplot(top_hits, aes(x=factor(cluster), y=estimate, fill=contrast)) +
  geom_col(position="dodge") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="gray40") +
  labs(y="Δ proportion (vs WT)", x="cluster",
       title="Clusters with significant proportion changes vs WT") +
  theme_classic()

# 5) save tables if you want
write.csv(grp_summary, file.path(save.location, "all_samples__cluster_props_mean_SE_by_genotype.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dunnett_by_cluster, file.path(save.location, "all_samples__cluster_prop_Dunnett_vs_WT_by_cluster.csv"), row.names = FALSE)



#to match the umap colors do the following




# Define your consistent cluster colors (matching your UMAP plots)
cluster_colors <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # L5/6 Excitatory PNs - Red
  "1" = "#377EB8",  # Striatal GABAergic (MSNs) - Blue
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs - Orange
  "3" = "#4DAF4A",  # Astrocytes / Radial Glia - Green
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+) - Purple
  "5" = "#A65628",  # Endothelial / Vascular - Brown
  "6" = "#66C2A5",  # OPCs / Immature Oligos - Teal
  "7" = "#999999",  # Microglia / Macrophages - Gray
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs - Yellow
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs - Light Purple
)

# Map numeric clusters to cell type names for the color palette
cluster_to_celltype <- c(
  "0" = "L5/6 Excitatory PNs",
  "1" = "Striatal GABAergic (MSNs)", 
  "2" = "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs",
  "3" = "Astrocytes / Radial Glia",
  "4" = "Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
  "5" = "Endothelial / Vascular",
  "6" = "OPCs / Immature Oligos",
  "7" = "Microglia / Macrophages",
  "8" = "L2/3 IT PNs",
  "9" = "L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs"
)

# Create cell type colors from cluster colors
celltype_colors <- cluster_colors
names(celltype_colors) <- cluster_to_celltype[names(cluster_colors)]

# Check what's in grp_summary
cat("grp_summary structure:\n")
print(str(grp_summary))
cat("Unique clusters in grp_summary:\n")
print(unique(grp_summary$cluster))

# Map genotypes to consistent names
genotype_mapping <- c(
  "WT" = "WT",
  "LD" = "LgDel", 
  "LD_NAC" = "LgDel_NAC",
  "WT_NAC" = "WT-NAC"
)

grp_summary$genotype_mapped <- genotype_mapping[as.character(grp_summary$genotype)]

# Define genotype order
desired_genotype_order <- c("WT-NAC", "LgDel_NAC", "LgDel", "WT")
grp_summary$genotype_mapped <- factor(grp_summary$genotype_mapped, levels = desired_genotype_order)

# Convert grp_summary cluster to factor with the same order as your Seurat object
# Use the same order as your celltype_ordered column
desired_celltype_order <- levels(all_samples$celltype_ordered)
cat("Desired cell type order:", paste(desired_celltype_order, collapse = ", "), "\n")

# Check if grp_summary uses numeric clusters or cell type names
if(is.numeric(grp_summary$cluster) || all(grp_summary$cluster %in% 0:9)) {
  # Convert numeric clusters to cell type names
  grp_summary$cluster_celltype <- cluster_to_celltype[as.character(grp_summary$cluster)]
  grp_summary$cluster_celltype <- factor(grp_summary$cluster_celltype, levels = desired_celltype_order)
  fill_column <- "cluster_celltype"
  fill_colors <- celltype_colors
  legend_name <- "Cell Type"
} else if(all(grp_summary$cluster %in% names(celltype_colors))) {
  # Already using cell type names
  grp_summary$cluster <- factor(grp_summary$cluster, levels = desired_celltype_order)
  fill_column <- "cluster"
  fill_colors <- celltype_colors
  legend_name <- "Cell Type"
} else {
  # Use as is
  fill_column <- "cluster"
  fill_colors <- Seurat::DiscretePalette(length(unique(grp_summary$cluster)), palette = "polychrome")
  legend_name <- "Cluster"
  warning("Cluster names in grp_summary don't match expected patterns. Using default colors.")
}

# Create the stacked bar plot with consistent colors
pdf("all_samples_Cluster_Composition_StackedBars_ConsistentColors.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
p <- ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype_mapped, y = mean_prop, fill = .data[[fill_column]])) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75, position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors, name = legend_name) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Cell Type Composition by Genotype",
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Composition (%)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 8)
  )

print(p)
dev.off()

cat("Stacked bar plot created with consistent colors!\n")
cat("Used color mapping:\n")
for(i in seq_along(celltype_colors)) {
  cat(names(celltype_colors)[i], "=", celltype_colors[i], "\n")
}
```
```{r all_samples cell proportion analysis across 5 focused cortical clusters (0, 2, 4, 8, 9) - ANOVA with Dunnett}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(multcomp)
library(purrr)

# 0) choose object + cluster column
obj <- all_samples                    
cluster_col <- "seurat_clusters"      

# 1) Filter to only clusters of interest (0, 2, 4, 8, 9)
clusters_of_interest <- c(0, 2, 4, 8, 9)

# Get only the clusters we're interested in
obj_filtered <- subset(obj, subset = seurat_clusters %in% clusters_of_interest)

# 2) per-animal proportions per cluster (within the 5 clusters only)
prop_per_animal <- obj_filtered@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(genotype, animal_id, !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(cluster = !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%  # Now sums to 1 within each animal for the 5 clusters
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 3) mean±SE per genotype (for plotting/reporting)
grp_summary <- prop_per_animal %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_prop = mean(prop),
    se = sd(prop) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
    n_animals = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 4) ANOVA + Dunnett (WT as reference) per cluster
run_one <- function(df, ref = "WT") {
  df$genotype <- stats::relevel(factor(df$genotype), ref = ref)
  fit <- aov(prop ~ genotype, data = df)
  anova_p <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

  dun <- multcomp::glht(fit, linfct = mcp(genotype = "Dunnett"))
  ds <- summary(dun)

  tibble::tibble(
    contrast = names(ds$test$coefficients),
    estimate = as.numeric(ds$test$coefficients),   # difference vs ref (WT)
    t        = as.numeric(ds$test$tstat),
    p_adj    = as.numeric(ds$test$pvalues),
    anova_p  = anova_p
  )
}

all_clusters <- sort(unique(prop_per_animal$cluster))

dunnett_by_cluster <- purrr::map_dfr(
  all_clusters,
  ~ run_one(dplyr::filter(prop_per_animal, cluster == .x), ref = "WT") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(cluster = .x),
  .id = NULL
) %>%
  dplyr::relocate(cluster, .before = contrast) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(contrast) %>%                      # BH adjust across clusters per contrast
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj_BH_across_clusters = p.adjust(p_adj, method = "BH")) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

## ----- 5) Visualizations with consistent colors -----

# Define colors for the 5 clusters (using your consistent color scheme)
cluster_colors_focused <- c(
  "0" = "#E41A1C",  # L5/6 Excitatory PNs - Red
  "2" = "#FF7F00",  # L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs - Orange
  "4" = "#984EA3",  # Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+) - Purple
  "8" = "#E6AB02",  # L2/3 IT PNs - Yellow
  "9" = "#CAB2D6"   # L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs - Light Purple
)

# Map cluster numbers to cell type names
cluster_to_celltype <- c(
  "0" = "L5/6 Excitatory PNs",
  "2" = "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs",
  "4" = "Cortical Interneurons (Lhx6+)",
  "8" = "L2/3 IT PNs",
  "9" = "L5/6 Corticothalamic PNs"
)

# Convert grp_summary cluster to cell type names for better labels
grp_summary$celltype <- cluster_to_celltype[as.character(grp_summary$cluster)]
grp_summary$celltype <- factor(grp_summary$celltype, levels = cluster_to_celltype)

# Map genotypes to consistent names
genotype_mapping <- c(
  "WT" = "WT",
  "LD" = "LgDel", 
  "LD_NAC" = "LgDel_NAC",
  "WT_NAC" = "WT-NAC"
)

grp_summary$genotype_mapped <- genotype_mapping[as.character(grp_summary$genotype)]
desired_genotype_order <- c("WT-NAC", "LgDel_NAC", "LgDel", "WT")
grp_summary$genotype_mapped <- factor(grp_summary$genotype_mapped, levels = desired_genotype_order)

# (a) Heatmap of mean proportions (%)
heat_df <- grp_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(pct = 100 * mean_prop)

pdf("focused_clusters_Proportion_Heatmap.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = celltype, y = genotype_mapped, fill = pct)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue", name = "% Cells") +
  labs(
    x = "Cell Type",
    y = "Genotype",
    title = "Focused Clusters: Proportion per Cell Type (Mean Across Animals)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
dev.off()

# (b) Faceted bars with mean ± SE
pdf("focused_clusters_Proportion_FacetedBars.pdf", width = 12, height = 8)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype_mapped, y = mean_prop)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_prop - se, ymax = mean_prop + se), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ celltype, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Proportion of Cells (Within 5 Clusters)",
    title = "Focused Clusters: Proportions by Genotype (Mean ± SE)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
dev.off()

# (c) Stacked column plot: cluster composition by genotype - FIXED
pdf("focused_clusters_Composition_StackedBars.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype_mapped, y = mean_prop, fill = celltype)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75, position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cluster_colors_focused, name = "Cell Type") +  # This was the problem!
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Focused Clusters: Cell Type Composition by Genotype",
    x = "Genotype", 
    y = "Composition (%)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )
dev.off()

# (d) Significant results plot
top_hits <- dunnett_by_cluster %>%
  filter(p_adj_BH_across_clusters < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(p_adj_BH_across_clusters)

# Add cell type names to dunnett results
dunnett_by_cluster$celltype <- cluster_to_celltype[as.character(dunnett_by_cluster$cluster)]

if(nrow(top_hits) > 0) {
  pdf("focused_clusters_Significant_Changes.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
  p <- ggplot(top_hits, aes(x = factor(cluster), y = estimate, fill = contrast)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray40") +
    labs(
      y = "Δ proportion (vs WT)", 
      x = "Cluster",
      title = "Focused Clusters: Significant Proportion Changes vs WT",
      subtitle = paste("BH-adjusted p < 0.1")
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  print(p)
  dev.off()
} else {
  cat("No significant results at BH-adjusted p < 0.1\n")
}

# 6) save tables
write.csv(grp_summary, "focused_clusters_props_mean_SE_by_genotype.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dunnett_by_cluster, "focused_clusters_prop_Dunnett_vs_WT_by_cluster.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Print summary
cat("\n=== FOCUSED CLUSTERS ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Clusters analyzed:", paste(clusters_of_interest, collapse = ", "), "\n")
cat("Corresponding cell types:\n")
for(clust in clusters_of_interest) {
  cat("  Cluster", clust, ":", cluster_to_celltype[as.character(clust)], "\n")
}
cat("Total animals:", length(unique(prop_per_animal$animal_id)), "\n")
cat("Significant results (BH < 0.1):", nrow(top_hits), "\n")



```
```{r all_samples cell proportion analysis across 2 UL % Lower layer as 100% - ANOVA with Dunnett Post-hoc}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(multcomp)
library(purrr)

# 0) choose object + cluster column
obj <- all_samples                    
cluster_col <- "seurat_clusters"      

# 1) Filter to only clusters of interest (0, 2, 4, 8, 9)
clusters_of_interest <- c(0, 2, 4, 8, 9)

# Get only the clusters we're interested in
obj_filtered <- subset(obj, subset = seurat_clusters %in% clusters_of_interest)

# 2) Calculate normalized proportions using lower layers (0,4,9) as reference
prop_per_animal_normalized <- obj_filtered@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(genotype, animal_id, !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(cluster = !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  # Pivot to wide format to calculate normalized proportions
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = cluster, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) %>%
  # Calculate reference population (clusters 0, 4, 9)
  dplyr::mutate(lower_layer_ref = `0` + `4` + `9`) %>%
  # Calculate normalized proportions for L2/3 clusters
  dplyr::mutate(
    cluster2_normalized = `2` / lower_layer_ref,
    cluster8_normalized = `8` / lower_layer_ref
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(genotype, animal_id, lower_layer_ref, cluster2_normalized, cluster8_normalized) %>%
  # Pivot back to long format for analysis
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(cluster2_normalized, cluster8_normalized),
    names_to = "cluster_type",
    values_to = "normalized_prop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    cluster = ifelse(cluster_type == "cluster2_normalized", "2", "8"),
    cluster = factor(cluster)
  )

# 3) mean±SE per genotype (for plotting/reporting)
normalized_summary <- prop_per_animal_normalized %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_norm_prop = mean(normalized_prop),
    se = sd(normalized_prop) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
    n_animals = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 4) ANOVA + Dunnett (WT as reference) for normalized proportions
run_one_normalized <- function(df, ref = "WT") {
  df$genotype <- stats::relevel(factor(df$genotype), ref = ref)
  fit <- aov(normalized_prop ~ genotype, data = df)
  anova_p <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

  dun <- multcomp::glht(fit, linfct = mcp(genotype = "Dunnett"))
  ds <- summary(dun)

  tibble::tibble(
    contrast = names(ds$test$coefficients),
    estimate = as.numeric(ds$test$coefficients),   # difference vs ref (WT)
    t        = as.numeric(ds$test$tstat),
    p_adj    = as.numeric(ds$test$pvalues),
    anova_p  = anova_p
  )
}

# Run analysis for each L2/3 cluster
dunnett_normalized <- purrr::map_dfr(
  c("2", "8"),
  ~ run_one_normalized(dplyr::filter(prop_per_animal_normalized, cluster == .x), ref = "WT") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(cluster = .x),
  .id = NULL
) %>%
  dplyr::relocate(cluster, .before = contrast) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(contrast) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj_BH_across_clusters = p.adjust(p_adj, method = "BH")) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

## ----- 5) Visualizations -----

# Define cell type names for clusters
cluster_to_celltype <- c(
  "2" = "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs",
  "8" = "L2/3 IT PNs"
)

# Map genotypes to consistent names
genotype_mapping <- c(
  "WT" = "WT",
  "LD" = "LgDel", 
  "LD_NAC" = "LgDel_NAC",
  "WT_NAC" = "WT-NAC"
)

normalized_summary$genotype_mapped <- genotype_mapping[as.character(normalized_summary$genotype)]
normalized_summary$celltype <- cluster_to_celltype[as.character(normalized_summary$cluster)]
desired_genotype_order <- c("WT-NAC", "LgDel_NAC", "LgDel", "WT")
normalized_summary$genotype_mapped <- factor(normalized_summary$genotype_mapped, levels = desired_genotype_order)

# Colors for L2/3 clusters
l23_colors <- c(
  "L2/3–4 Excitatory PNs" = "#FF7F00",  # Orange
  "L2/3 IT PNs" = "#E6AB02"             # Yellow
)

# (a) Bar plot of normalized proportions
pdf("UL__normalized_proportions.pdf", width = 10, height = 6)
ggplot(normalized_summary, aes(x = genotype_mapped, y = mean_norm_prop, fill = celltype)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_norm_prop - se, ymax = mean_norm_prop + se), 
                position = position_dodge(0.9), width = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = l23_colors, name = "Cell Type") +
  labs(
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Normalized Proportion (L2/3 ÷ Lower Layers)",
    title = "L2/3 Proportions Normalized to Lower Layer Neurons (0+4+9)",
    subtitle = "Reference: Clusters 0 (L5/6 Excitatory) + 4 (Interneurons) + 9 (L5/6 Corticothalamic)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
dev.off()

# (b) Faceted by cluster
pdf("UL_normalized_faceted.pdf", width = 12, height = 6)
ggplot(normalized_summary, aes(x = genotype_mapped, y = mean_norm_prop, fill = celltype)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_norm_prop - se, ymax = mean_norm_prop + se), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ celltype, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = l23_colors) +
  labs(
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Normalized Proportion (L2/3 ÷ Lower Layers)",
    title = "L2/3 Proportions Normalized to Lower Layer Neurons"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
dev.off()


# (d) Significant results plot
top_hits_normalized <- dunnett_normalized %>%
  filter(p_adj_BH_across_clusters < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(p_adj_BH_across_clusters)

# Add cell type names
dunnett_normalized$celltype <- cluster_to_celltype[as.character(dunnett_normalized$cluster)]

if(nrow(top_hits_normalized) > 0) {
  pdf("UL_normalized_significant_changes.pdf", width = 8, height = 6)
  p <- ggplot(top_hits_normalized, aes(x = celltype, y = estimate, fill = contrast)) +
    geom_col(position = "dodge") +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "gray40") +
    labs(
      y = "Δ normalized proportion (vs WT)", 
      x = "L2/3 Cluster",
      title = "L2/3 Changes Relative to Lower Layers",
      subtitle = "Normalized to clusters 0+4+9; BH-adjusted p < 0.1"
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
  print(p)
  dev.off()
} else {
  cat("No significant results at BH-adjusted p < 0.1\n")
}

# 6) save tables
write.csv(normalized_summary, "UL_normalized_proportions_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dunnett_normalized, "UL_normalized_Dunnett_results.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Print summary
cat("\n=== L2/3 NORMALIZED ANALYSIS SUMMARY ===\n")
cat("Reference clusters (lower layers): 0, 4, 9\n")
cat("Tested clusters (L2/3): 2, 8\n")
cat("Calculation: (L2/3 cell count) ÷ (Cluster 0 + 4 + 9 cell count)\n")
cat("Total animals:", length(unique(prop_per_animal_normalized$animal_id)), "\n")
cat("Significant results (BH < 0.1):", nrow(top_hits_normalized), "\n")

# Also print the actual normalized values for inspection
cat("\nNormalized proportion means:\n")
print(normalized_summary)





```

```{r subset to l23 cells using gating method}
plan("multisession", workers = 12)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 150 * 1024^3)  # optional for big jobs



DefaultAssay(all_samples) <- "Xenium"

# (optional sanity check)
#all_samples <- JoinLayers(all_samples)  # only if you loaded raw multi-layer data
#all_samples <- NormalizeData(all_samples, assay = "Xenium")

# Check that data layer was created
Layers(all_samples[["Xenium"]])

# Check range of normalized values
summary(GetAssayData(all_samples, assay = "Xenium", slot = "data")["Cux2", ])



# now you can subset your L2/3 cells
gate_ids <- WhichCells(
  all_samples,
  expression = Cux2 > 0.5 & Cux1 > 0.5 & Pou3f2 > 0.5 & Rasgrf2 > 0.5 & Gad1 < 0.5 & Gad2 < 0.5 & Foxp2 < 0.5 & Fezf2 < 0.5
)
l23 <- subset(all_samples, cells = gate_ids)


length(gate_ids)
table(l23$genotype)
table(l23$animal_id)

```
```{r l23 sct umap pca clustreing excluding 22q11 genes}
plan("multisession", workers = 12)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 150 * 1024^3)  # optional for big jobs

DefaultAssay(l23) <- "Xenium"

# --------------------------
# 1. SCTransform normalization
# --------------------------
l23 <- SCTransform(
  l23,
  assay = "Xenium",
  method = "glmGamPoi",
  vars.to.regress = "nCount_Xenium",
  verbose = TRUE
)

# --------------------------
# 2. Define and remove 22q11 genes from HVGs
# --------------------------
x22q11_genes <- c(
  "Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt",
  "Tango2", "Mrpl40", "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8",
  "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh"
)

available_22q11 <- intersect(x22q11_genes, VariableFeatures(l23))
VariableFeatures(l23) <- setdiff(VariableFeatures(l23), available_22q11)

cat("Removed", length(available_22q11), "22q11 genes from HVGs for clustering\n")

# --------------------------
# 3. Dimensional reduction & integration
# --------------------------
l23 <- RunPCA(l23, npcs = 50, verbose = FALSE)
ElbowPlot(l23)
# suggest PCs capturing ~90% variance
pca_var <- all_samples[["pca"]]@stdev^2
pca_var_perc <- pca_var / sum(pca_var) * 100
suggested_pcs <- which(cumsum(pca_var_perc) >= 80)[1]
cat("Suggested PCs:", suggested_pcs, "\n")

l23 <- RunHarmony(
  object = l23,
  group.by.vars = "animal_id",
  dims.use = 1:21,     # adjust based on elbow
  verbose = TRUE
)

l23 <- RunUMAP(l23, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:21)
l23 <- FindNeighbors(l23, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:21)
l23 <- FindClusters(l23, resolution = 0.1)

# --------------------------
# 4. Save for downstream use
# --------------------------
l23$genotype  <- factor(l23$genotype)
l23$animal_id <- factor(l23$animal_id)
saveRDS(l23, file.path(save.location, "L23_no22q11_SCT_Harmony_processed.rds"))

# --------------------------
# 5. Quick check: ensure exclusion worked
# --------------------------
any(x22q11_genes %in% rownames(Loadings(l23[["pca"]])))
# should return FALSE if they’re excluded

```

```{r l23 cluster and spatil plot}
# For RStudio, use multisession instead of multicore
plan("multisession", workers = 12)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 150 * 1024^3)  # 150GB

setwd(save.location)
l23 <- readRDS(file.path(save.location, "L23_no22q11_SCT_Harmony_processed.rds"))

# Spatial plot showing only L2/3 cells
sp_l23 <- ImageDimPlot(
  l23,
  group.by = "seurat_clusters",
  axes = FALSE,
  size = 0.8
) + ggtitle("L2/3 Cells - Spatial Distribution")

ggsave("L23_no22q11_spatial_genotype.pdf", sp_l23, width = 10, height = 8)

# You might want to add a quick QC check:
cat("Original cells:", ncol(all_samples), "\n")
cat("L2/3 subset:", ncol(l23), "\n") 
cat("Percentage kept:", round(ncol(l23)/ncol(all_samples)*100, 1), "%\n")


# And verify the gate worked by checking marker expression:
FeaturePlot(l23, features = c("Cux2", "Gad1"), order = TRUE)


# Reorder the genotype factor levels in the metadata
l23$genotype <- factor(l23$genotype, levels = c("WT", "LD", "LD_NAC", "WT_NAC"))

# Generate the violin plot with the new order
vln_plot <- VlnPlot(l23, 
                    features = c("Cux1", "Cux2", "Pou3f2", "Rasgrf2", "Slc17a7", "Bcl11b"), 
                    group.by = "genotype", 
                    pt.size = 0)

# Save as PDF
ggsave("L23_no22q11_violin_plot_markers.pdf", vln_plot, width = 16, height = 12, dpi = 300)



# --------------------------
# 5. Quick check: ensure exclusion worked
# --------------------------
any(x22q11_genes %in% rownames(Loadings(l23[["pca"]])))
# should return FALSE if they’re excluded




p1 <- DimPlot(l23, group.by = "animal_id", shuffle = TRUE) + ggtitle("By Animal ID")
p2 <- DimPlot(l23, group.by = "genotype") + ggtitle("By Genotype")
p3 <- DimPlot(l23, group.by = "seurat_clusters") + ggtitle("Clusters")
p1 + p2 + p3
ggsave("L23_no22q11_umap_animal_id.pdf", p1, width = 10, height = 8)
ggsave("L23_no22q11_umap_genotype.pdf", p2, width = 10, height = 8)
ggsave("L23_no22q11_umap_clusters.pdf", p3, width = 10, height = 8)



# Spatial plot of clusters
sp1 <- ImageDimPlot(l23, group.by = "seurat_clusters", axes = FALSE) + 
  ggtitle("Spatial Clusters")

# Spatial plot by genotype  
sp2 <- ImageDimPlot(l23, group.by = "genotype", axes = FALSE) + 
  ggtitle("Spatial Genotype")

# Spatial plot by animal_id
sp3 <- ImageDimPlot(l23, group.by = "animal_id", axes = FALSE) + 
  ggtitle("Spatial Animal ID")


ggsave("L23_no22q11_sptial_animal_id.pdf", sp3, width = 10, height = 8)
ggsave("L23_no22q11_sptial_genotype.pdf", sp2, width = 10, height = 8)
ggsave("L23_no22q11_sptial_clusters.pdf", sp1, width = 10, height = 8)


# Add L2/3 identity to metadata
all_samples$is_L23 <- ifelse(colnames(all_samples) %in% colnames(l23), "L2/3", "Other")

# Plot highlighting L2/3 cells
p <- DimPlot(all_samples, group.by = "is_L23", cols = c("#FF7F00", "gray90"), pt.size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("L2/3 Cells in Full Dataset")
print(p)
ggsave("L23_no22q11_umap_highlighted.pdf", p, width = 10, height = 8)





# Create a mapping between animal_id and image names
image_mapping <- list(
  "WT_5" = "X1xWT_929",      # Assuming WT_5 corresponds to X4xWT_270
  "LD_3" = "X5xLD_270",      # Assuming LD_3 corresponds to X5xLD_270  
  "LD_NAC_5" = "X5xLD_NAC_929",  # LD_NAC_5 corresponds to X5xLD_NAC_929
  "WT_NAC_1" = "X3xWT_NAC_929"   # WT_NAC_1 corresponds to X3xWT_NAC_929
)

# Verify these mappings make sense by checking cell counts
print("Verifying mappings:")
for(animal_id in names(image_mapping)) {
  image_name <- image_mapping[[animal_id]]
  cells_in_image <- Cells(l23)[l23$animal_id == animal_id]
  print(paste(animal_id, "->", image_name, ":", length(cells_in_image), "cells"))
}

# Now plot each replicate with the correct image
replicates <- c("WT_5", "LD_3", "LD_NAC_5", "WT_NAC_1")

for (rep in replicates) {
  image_name <- image_mapping[[rep]]
  
  # Subset to cells from this specific replicate
  cells_use <- Cells(l23)[l23$animal_id == rep]
  
  if (length(cells_use) > 0) {
    print(paste("Plotting", rep, "using image", image_name, "with", length(cells_use), "cells"))
    
    # Create the plot specifying the correct image
    p <- ImageDimPlot(l23, 
                     cells = cells_use,
                     fov = image_name,  # Specify the correct FOV/image
                     group.by = "seurat_clusters", 
                     axes = FALSE, 
                     dark.background = FALSE) + 
      ggtitle(paste("Spatial Clusters -", rep))
    
    # Save each plot
    ggsave(paste0("spatial_clusters_", rep, ".pdf"), p, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 300)
    print(paste("Saved plot for", rep))
  } else {
    print(paste("No cells found for", rep))
  }
}




# Subset each replicate to its specific section
ld_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = animal_id == "LD_3")
wt_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = animal_id == "WT_5")
ld_nac_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = animal_id == "LD_NAC_5")
wt_nac_sample <- subset(all_samples, subset = animal_id == "WT_NAC_1")

# Create a list of all samples for easy processing
samples_list <- list(
  "LD" = ld_sample,
  "WT" = wt_sample,
  "LD_NAC" = ld_nac_sample,
  "WT_NAC" = wt_nac_sample
)

# Print cell counts for each sample
print("Cell counts per sample:")
for (sample_name in names(samples_list)) {
  n_cells <- ncol(samples_list[[sample_name]])
  n_l23 <- sum(Cells(samples_list[[sample_name]]) %in% Cells(l23))
  print(paste(sample_name, ":", n_cells, "total cells,", n_l23, "L2/3 cells"))
}

# Create spatial plots for each sample showing L2/3 cells
for (sample_name in names(samples_list)) {
  sample_obj <- samples_list[[sample_name]]
  
  # Add L2/3 highlight column
  sample_obj$l23_highlight <- ifelse(
    Cells(sample_obj) %in% Cells(l23),
    "L2/3",
    "Other"
  )
  
  tryCatch({
    # Create the spatial plot
    p <- ImageDimPlot(sample_obj,
                     group.by = "l23_highlight",
                     cols = c("L2/3" = "#FF7F00", "Other" = "lightgray"),
                     axes = FALSE,
                     dark.background = FALSE,
                     size = 0.8) + 
      ggtitle(paste(sample_name, "L2/3 Cells")) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))
    
    # Save the plot
    ggsave(paste0("L23_", sample_name, "_spatial.pdf"), 
           p, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
    print(paste("Saved spatial plot for", sample_name))
    
  }, error = function(e) {
    print(paste("Error creating plot for", sample_name, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# Create a combined figure with all samples
print("Creating combined figure...")

# Create individual plots for each sample
plot_list <- list()
for (sample_name in names(samples_list)) {
  sample_obj <- samples_list[[sample_name]]
  
  # Add L2/3 highlight column
  sample_obj$l23_highlight <- ifelse(
    Cells(sample_obj) %in% Cells(l23),
    "L2/3",
    "Other"
  )
  
  tryCatch({
    p <- ImageDimPlot(sample_obj,
                     group.by = "l23_highlight",
                     cols = c("L2/3" = "#FF7F00", "Other" = "lightgray"),
                     axes = FALSE,
                     dark.background = FALSE,
                     size = 0.6) + 
      ggtitle(sample_name) +
      theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12))
    
    plot_list[[sample_name]] <- p
  }, error = function(e) {
    print(paste("Error creating plot for", sample_name, "in combined figure:", e$message))
  })
}

# Combine all plots into one figure
if (length(plot_list) > 0) {
  combined_plot <- wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 2)
  ggsave("L23_all_samples_combined.pdf", combined_plot, width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300)
  print("Saved combined plot with all samples")
}

# Print final summary
print("=== FINAL SUMMARY ===")
for (sample_name in names(samples_list)) {
  sample_obj <- samples_list[[sample_name]]
  n_total <- ncol(sample_obj)
  n_l23 <- sum(Cells(sample_obj) %in% Cells(l23))
  print(paste(sample_name, ":", n_l23, "L2/3 cells out of", n_total, "total cells", 
              "(", round(n_l23/n_total*100, 1), "%)"))
}
















# Subset to only cluster 2
cluster_2 <- subset(all_samples, subset = seurat_clusters == 2)
cluster_5 <- subset(all_samples, subset = seurat_clusters == 5)
# Check the result
print(paste("Number of cells in cluster 2:", ncol(cluster_2)))
print("Cluster distribution in the subset:")
print(table(cluster_2$seurat_clusters))

# Add cluster 2 identity to metadata
all_samples$is_cluster_2 <- ifelse(all_samples$seurat_clusters == 2, "Cluster_2", "Other")

# Create UMAP plot highlighting cluster 2
p_umap <- DimPlot(all_samples, 
                  group.by = "is_cluster_2", 
                  cols = c("Cluster_2" = "#FF7F00", "Other" = "gray90"), 
                  pt.size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Cluster 2 in Full Dataset")
print(p_umap)
ggsave("cluster_2_umap_highlighted.pdf", p_umap, width = 10, height = 8)

# Create spatial plot highlighting cluster 2
p_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(all_samples,
                         group.by = "is_cluster_2",
                         cols = c("Cluster_2" = "#FF7F00", "Other" = "lightgray"),
                         axes = FALSE,
                         dark.background = FALSE,
                         size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Cluster 2 - Spatial Distribution")
print(p_spatial)
ggsave("cluster_2_spatial_highlighted.pdf", p_spatial, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Optional: Create individual spatial plots for each section
for (section in Images(all_samples)) {
  tryCatch({
    p_section <- ImageDimPlot(all_samples,
                             fov = section,
                             group.by = "is_cluster_2",
                             cols = c("Cluster_2" = "#FF7F00", "Other" = "lightgray"),
                             axes = FALSE,
                             dark.background = FALSE,
                             size = 0.6) +
      ggtitle(paste("Cluster 2 -", section))
    
    ggsave(paste0("cluster_2_", section, "_spatial.pdf"), 
           p_section, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
    print(paste("Saved spatial plot for section:", section))
  }, error = function(e) {
    print(paste("Error with section", section, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# Print summary statistics
print("Summary of cluster 2:")
cluster_counts <- table(all_samples$is_cluster_2)
print(cluster_counts)
print(paste("Percentage of cells in cluster 2:", 
            round(cluster_counts["Cluster_2"] / sum(cluster_counts) * 100, 2), "%"))

# Check if there are actually cells labeled as cluster 2 in the endothelial region
# Let's look at the cluster composition more carefully
print("Detailed cluster distribution:")
print(table(all_samples$seurat_clusters))



endothelial_markers <- c("Pecam1", "Cldn5", "Kdr") # Common endothelial markers
FeaturePlot(all_samples, features = endothelial_markers, pt.size = 0.5)
DimPlot(all_samples,
                 reduction = "pca",
                 group.by = "is_cluster_2",
                 cols = c("Cluster_2" = "#FF7F00", "Other" = "gray90"),
                 pt.size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Cluster 2 - PCA")

# Create a visualization of key drivers
library(ggplot2)

# Prepare data for visualization
pc1_genes <- data.frame(
  gene = names(top_pc1)[1:10],
  loading = top_pc1[1:10],
  pc = "PC1"
)

pc2_genes <- data.frame(
  gene = names(top_pc2)[1:10],
  loading = top_pc2[1:10],
  pc = "PC2"
)

p_loadings <- ggplot(rbind(pc1_genes, pc2_genes), 
                     aes(x = reorder(gene, abs(loading)), y = abs(loading), fill = loading > 0)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~pc, scales = "free_y") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "Gene", y = "Absolute Loading Value",
       title = "Key Genes Driving Principal Components",
       subtitle = "PC1: ECM/Vascular | PC2: Microglia vs Neuronal") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("FALSE" = "blue", "TRUE" = "red"),
                    name = "Direction",
                    labels = c("Negative", "Positive")) +
  theme_minimal()

ggsave("pca_loadings_analysis.pdf", p_loadings, width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300)

















# Create enhanced PCA plot for publication
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)

# Get variance explained
variance_explained <- all_samples[["pca"]]@stdev^2 / sum(all_samples[["pca"]]@stdev^2) * 100

# Create clean PCA plot
p_pca_clean <- DimPlot(all_samples,
                      reduction = "pca",
                      group.by = "is_cluster_2",
                      cols = c("Cluster_2" = "#FF7F00", "Other" = "gray90"),
                      pt.size = 1.5,
                      order = c("Cluster_2")) +  # Plot Cluster_2 on top
  xlab(paste0("PC1 (", round(variance_explained[1], 1), "% variance)")) +
  ylab(paste0("PC2 (", round(variance_explained[2], 1), "% variance)")) +
  ggtitle("PCA - Cluster 2 Spatial Distribution") +
  theme_cowplot() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14),
        legend.position = "right",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 12))

# Add annotations to highlight key findings
p_pca_annotated <- p_pca_clean + 
  annotate("text", x = -50, y = -40, 
           label = "Cluster 2 occupies\ndistinct terminal\nposition", 
           hjust = 0, size = 4, color = "#FF7F00", fontface = "bold")

print(p_pca_annotated)
ggsave("figure_3A_cluster2_pca.pdf", p_pca_annotated, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)





```
```{r cluster 5 endothelial cells pca}

# Subset to only cluster 5
cluster_5 <- subset(all_samples, subset = seurat_clusters == 5)

# Check the result
print(paste("Number of cells in cluster 5:", ncol(cluster_5)))
print("Cluster distribution in the subset:")
print(table(cluster_5$seurat_clusters))

# Add cluster 5 identity to metadata
all_samples$is_cluster_5 <- ifelse(all_samples$seurat_clusters == 5, "Cluster_5", "Other")

# Create UMAP plot highlighting cluster 5
p_umap_5 <- DimPlot(all_samples, 
                   group.by = "is_cluster_5", 
                   cols = c("Cluster_5" = "#377EB8", "Other" = "gray90"),  # Blue color for cluster 5
                   pt.size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Cluster 5 in Full Dataset")
print(p_umap_5)
ggsave("cluster_5_umap_highlighted.pdf", p_umap_5, width = 10, height = 8)

# Create spatial plot highlighting cluster 5
p_spatial_5 <- ImageDimPlot(all_samples,
                           group.by = "is_cluster_5",
                           cols = c("Cluster_5" = "#377EB8", "Other" = "lightgray"),  # Blue color
                           axes = FALSE,
                           dark.background = FALSE,
                           size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Cluster 5 - Spatial Distribution")
print(p_spatial_5)
ggsave("cluster_5_spatial_highlighted.pdf", p_spatial_5, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Optional: Create individual spatial plots for each section
for (section in Images(all_samples)) {
  tryCatch({
    p_section_5 <- ImageDimPlot(all_samples,
                               fov = section,
                               group.by = "is_cluster_5",
                               cols = c("Cluster_5" = "#377EB8", "Other" = "lightgray"),
                               axes = FALSE,
                               dark.background = FALSE,
                               size = 0.6) +
      ggtitle(paste("Cluster 5 -", section))
    
    ggsave(paste0("cluster_5_", section, "_spatial.pdf"), 
           p_section_5, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300)
    print(paste("Saved spatial plot for section:", section))
  }, error = function(e) {
    print(paste("Error with section", section, ":", e$message))
  })
}

# Print summary statistics
print("Summary of cluster 5:")
cluster_counts_5 <- table(all_samples$is_cluster_5)
print(cluster_counts_5)
print(paste("Percentage of cells in cluster 5:", 
            round(cluster_counts_5["Cluster_5"] / sum(cluster_counts_5) * 100, 2), "%"))

# Create PCA plot highlighting cluster 5
p_pca_5 <- DimPlot(all_samples,
                  reduction = "pca",
                  group.by = "is_cluster_5",
                  cols = c("Cluster_5" = "#377EB8", "Other" = "gray90"),
                  pt.size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Cluster 5 - PCA")
print(p_pca_5)
ggsave("cluster_5_pca.pdf", p_pca_5, width = 10, height = 8)

# Compare cluster 2 and cluster 5 side by side
p_compare <- DimPlot(all_samples,
                    group.by = "seurat_clusters",
                    cols = c("2" = "#FF7F00", "5" = "#377EB8"),  # Highlight only clusters 2 and 5
                    cells.highlight = Cells(cluster_2),  # Cluster 2 cells
                    cols.highlight = c("2" = "#FF7F00"),
                    sizes.highlight = 0.8) +
  ggtitle("Clusters 2 and 5 Comparison") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("2" = "#FF7F00", "5" = "#377EB8", "Other" = "gray90"))

print(p_compare)
ggsave("clusters_2_5_comparison.pdf", p_compare, width = 10, height = 8)

# Create a combined spatial plot showing both clusters
all_samples$clusters_2_5 <- case_when(
  all_samples$seurat_clusters == 2 ~ "Cluster_2",
  all_samples$seurat_clusters == 5 ~ "Cluster_5",
  TRUE ~ "Other"
)

p_spatial_combined <- ImageDimPlot(all_samples,
                                  group.by = "clusters_2_5",
                                  cols = c("Cluster_2" = "#FF7F00", "Cluster_5" = "#377EB8", "Other" = "lightgray"),
                                  axes = FALSE,
                                  dark.background = FALSE,
                                  size = 0.5) +
  ggtitle("Clusters 2 and 5 - Spatial Distribution")
print(p_spatial_combined)
ggsave("clusters_2_5_spatial_combined.pdf", p_spatial_combined, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300)

# Print comparative statistics
print("=== COMPARATIVE SUMMARY ===")
print(paste("Cluster 2 cells:", sum(all_samples$seurat_clusters == 2)))
print(paste("Cluster 5 cells:", sum(all_samples$seurat_clusters == 5)))
print(paste("Ratio (Cluster 2 / Cluster 5):", 
            round(sum(all_samples$seurat_clusters == 2) / sum(all_samples$seurat_clusters == 5), 2)))
```

```{r find subcluster markers for l23 subset}
plan("multisession", workers = 12)
options(future.globals.maxSize = 150 * 1024^3)  # 150GB

DefaultAssay(l23) <- "Xenium"
all_samples <- JoinLayers(l23)
all_samples <- NormalizeData(l23, normalization.method = "LogNormalize")
all_samples <- ScaleData(l23)


# Find markers with slightly relaxed parameters
AllMarkers <- FindAllMarkers(
    l23,
    assay = "Xenium",
    only.pos = TRUE,
    min.pct = 0.25,           # Reduced from 0.25
    logfc.threshold = 0.25,   # Reduced from 0.25
    test.use = "wilcox"      # Use Wilcoxon test
)

# save for reference
write.csv(AllMarkers, file.path(save.location, "L23_no2211_Xenium_FindAllMarkers_res0.1_dims21.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r DE using findmarkersof l23 subset}
# Set identities to genotype for DE analysis
Idents(l23) <- "genotype"
# Include animal_id as latent variable to control for batch effects
de_wt_vs_ld <- FindMarkers(
  l23,
  ident.1 = "LD",
  ident.2 = "WT",
  latent.vars = "animal_id",  # Controls for replicate effects
  min.pct = 0.1,
  logfc.threshold = 0.1
)
# Include animal_id as latent variable to control for batch effects
de_wt_vs_ldnac <- FindMarkers(
  l23,
  ident.1 = "LD_NAC",
  ident.2 = "WT",
  latent.vars = "animal_id",  # Controls for replicate effects
  min.pct = 0.1,
  logfc.threshold = 0.1
)
# Include animal_id as latent variable to control for batch effects
de_ld_vs_ldnac <- FindMarkers(
  l23,
  ident.1 = "LD_NAC",
  ident.2 = "LD",
  latent.vars = "animal_id",  # Controls for replicate effects
  min.pct = 0.1,
  logfc.threshold = 0.1
)
# Include animal_id as latent variable to control for batch effects
de_wt_vs_wtnac <- FindMarkers(
  l23,
  ident.1 = "WT_NAC",
  ident.2 = "WT",
  latent.vars = "animal_id",  # Controls for replicate effects
  min.pct = 0.1,
  logfc.threshold = 0.1
)
# Include animal_id as latent variable to control for batch effects
de_wtnac_vs_ldnac <- FindMarkers(
  l23,
  ident.1 = "LD_NAC",
  ident.2 = "WT_NAC",
  latent.vars = "animal_id",  # Controls for replicate effects
  min.pct = 0.1,
  logfc.threshold = 0.1
)
# Save all DE results
write.csv(de_wt_vs_ld, "L23-22q_WT_vs_LD_DE_Findmarkers.csv")
write.csv(de_wt_vs_ldnac, "L23-22q_WT_vs_LDNAC_DE_Findmarkers.csv")
write.csv(de_ld_vs_ldnac, "L23-22q_LD_vs_LDNAC_DE_Findmarkers.csv")
write.csv(de_wt_vs_wtnac, "L23-22q_WT_vs_WTNAC_DE_Findmarkers.csv")
write.csv(de_wtnac_vs_ldnac, "L23-22q_WTNAC_vs_LDNAC_DE_Findmarkers.csv")

# Volcano plots
EnhancedVolcano::EnhancedVolcano(de_wt_vs_ldnac,
  lab = rownames(de_wt_vs_ldnac),
  x = 'avg_log2FC',
  y = 'p_val_adj',
  title = 'WT vs LD in L2/3'
)

volcano <- function(df, title) {
  df$Signif <- ifelse(df$p_val_adj < 0.05 & abs(df$avg_log2FC) > 0.25, "Yes", "No")
  ggplot(df, aes(x = avg_log2FC, y = -log10(p_val), color = Signif)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.25, 0.25), linetype = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "gray70", "Yes" = "red3")) +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)") +
    theme_classic()
}

volcano(de_wt_vs_ld, "L2/3 PNs: LD vs WT")
```
```{r volcano plots for FindMarkers DE analysis}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)

create_volcano <- function(de_results, title, p_cutoff = 0.05, fc_cutoff = 0.25) {
  # Prepare data
  volcano_data <- de_results %>%
    mutate(
      gene = rownames(.),
      log10_pval = -log10(p_val_adj),
      significance = case_when(
        p_val_adj < p_cutoff & avg_log2FC > fc_cutoff ~ "Up",
        p_val_adj < p_cutoff & avg_log2FC < -fc_cutoff ~ "Down", 
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      )
    )
  
  # Get top genes for labeling
  top_genes <- volcano_data %>%
    filter(significance != "Not significant") %>%
    arrange(p_val_adj) %>%
    head(10)
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(volcano_data, aes(x = avg_log2FC, y = log10_pval, color = significance)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(p_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-fc_cutoff, fc_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = top_genes,
      aes(label = gene),
      size = 3,
      max.overlaps = 20,
      box.padding = 0.5,
      segment.color = "gray50"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "Up" = "#E31A1C",
        "Down" = "#1F78B4", 
        "Not significant" = "gray70"
      )
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Log2 Fold Change",
      y = "-Log10 Adjusted P-value"
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(p)
}


# Create and save each volcano plot
p1 <- create_volcano(de_wt_vs_ld, "LD vs WT in L2/3")
p2 <- create_volcano(de_wt_vs_ldnac, "LD_NAC vs WT in L2/3")
p3 <- create_volcano(de_ld_vs_ldnac, "LD_NAC vs LD in L2/3")
p4 <- create_volcano(de_wt_vs_wtnac, "WT_NAC vs WT in L2/3")
p5 <- create_volcano(de_wtnac_vs_ldnac, "LD_NAC vs WT_NAC in L2/3")

# Save individual plots
ggsave("no22q11_l23_volcano_LD_vs_WT.pdf", p1, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("no22q11_l23_volcano_LDNAC_vs_WT.pdf", p2, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("no22q11_l23_volcano_LDNAC_vs_LD.pdf", p3, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("no22q11_l23_volcano_WTNAC_vs_WT.pdf", p4, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("no22q11_l23_volcano_LDNAC_vs_WTNAC.pdf", p5, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```
```{r ANOVA on individual gene across conditions}


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

gene <- "Nyap2"  # change if needed

# make sure we're using Xenium counts for a RNAscope-like metric
DefaultAssay(l23) <- "Xenium"
l23 <- JoinLayers(l23)
# Set identities to genotype for DE analysis
Idents(l23) <- "genotype"
# check gene exists
if (!gene %in% rownames(l23)) stop(paste("Gene not in panel:", gene))

# pull per-cell expression + metadata
df <- data.frame(
  expr = as.numeric(GetAssayData(l23, slot = "counts")[gene, colnames(l23)]),
  animal_id = l23$animal_id,
  genotype  = l23$genotype
)

# per-animal means
rep_means <- df %>%
  group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  summarize(mean_expr = mean(expr, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# group summary for plotting
grp_sum <- rep_means %>%
  group_by(genotype) %>%
  summarize(
    mean = mean(mean_expr),
    se   = sd(mean_expr) / sqrt(n()),
    n    = n(), .groups = "drop"
  )

# ANOVA + Tukey
fit <- aov(mean_expr ~ genotype, data = rep_means)
anova_res <- summary(fit)
tukey_res <- TukeyHSD(fit)

print(anova_res)
print(tukey_res)

ggplot(rep_means, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_expr)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se),
    width = 0.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean),
    size = 3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = paste(gene, "per-animal mean (L2/3 PNs)"),
    y = "mean counts per cell",
    x = "genotype"
  ) +
  theme_classic()


```
```{r ANOVA with cells with zero counts removed}

thr <- 0  # >0 counts = positive; tweak if you want a higher puncta threshold

rep_pos <- df %>%
  group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  summarize(pct_pos = mean(expr > thr) * 100, .groups = "drop")

# ANOVA + Tukey on % positive
fit_pos <- aov(pct_pos ~ genotype, data = rep_pos)
print(summary(fit_pos))
print(TukeyHSD(fit_pos))

# plot
grp_sum_pos <- rep_pos %>%
  group_by(genotype) %>%
  summarize(mean = mean(pct_pos), se = sd(pct_pos)/sqrt(n()), n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(rep_means, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_expr)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se),
    width = 0.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean),
    size = 3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = paste(gene, "per-animal mean (L2/3 PNs)"),
    y = "mean counts per cell",
    x = "genotype"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

```
```{r DE analysis with count aggregate in DESeq2}

pb <- AggregateExpression(
  l23,
  assays = "Xenium",
  group.by = c("genotype", "animal_id"),
  slot = "counts"
)
counts <- pb$Xenium

meta <- data.frame(
  genotype  = sub("_.*", "", colnames(counts)),
  animal_id = sub("^[^_]*_", "", colnames(counts))
)
meta$genotype  <- gsub("-", "_", meta$genotype)
meta$animal_id <- gsub("-", "_", meta$animal_id)


meta$genotype <- factor(meta$genotype, levels = c("WT", "LD", "LD_NAC", "WT_NAC"))

library(DESeq2)

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = round(counts),
  colData = meta,
  design = ~ genotype
)

dds <- DESeq(dds)

resultsNames(dds)

res_LD_vs_WT      <- results(dds, name = "genotype_LD_vs_WT")
res_LD_NAC_vs_WT  <- results(dds, name = "genotype_LD_NAC_vs_WT")
res_WT_NAC_vs_WT  <- results(dds, name = "genotype_WT_NAC_vs_WT")

# for non-reference contrasts (optional):
res_LD_NAC_vs_LD  <- results(dds, contrast = c("genotype", "LD_NAC", "LD"))
res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC <- results(dds, contrast = c("genotype", "WT_NAC", "LD_NAC"))


write.csv(as.data.frame(res_LD_vs_WT),      file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_DESeq2_LD_vs_WT.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_LD_NAC_vs_WT),  file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_DESeq2_LD_NAC_vs_WT.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_WT_NAC_vs_WT),  file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_DESeq2_WT_NAC_vs_WT.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_LD_NAC_vs_LD),  file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_DESeq2_LD_NAC_vs_LD.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC), file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_DESeq2_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))

library(ggplot2)
volcano <- function(df, title) {
  df$Signif <- ifelse(df$padj < 0.05 & abs(df$log2FoldChange) > 0.25, "Yes", "No")
  ggplot(df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(pvalue), color = Signif)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.25, 0.25), linetype = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "gray70", "Yes" = "red3")) +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)") +
    theme_classic()
}

volcano(res_LD_vs_WT, "L2/3 PNs: LD vs WT")
volcano(res_LD_NAC_vs_WT, "L2/3 PNs: LD_NAC vs WT")
volcano(res_WT_NAC_vs_WT, "L2/3 PNs: WT_NAC vs WT")


```
```{r DE analysis with count aggregate in Limma}

pb <- AggregateExpression(
  l23,
  assays = "SCT",
  group.by = c("genotype", "animal_id"),  # aggregate per replicate within each genotype
  slot = "data"   # or "counts" for raw sums
)



# assign the matrix properly
pb_mat <- pb$SCT  # this is the pseudobulk (aggregated) expression matrix
meta <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, strsplit(colnames(pb_mat), "_")))
colnames(meta) <- c("genotype", "animal_id")

meta$genotype <- gsub("-", "_", meta$genotype)
meta$genotype <- factor(meta$genotype)

design <- model.matrix(~0 + genotype, data = meta)
colnames(design) <- levels(meta$genotype)
colnames(design)


fit <- lmFit(pb_mat, design)
contr <- makeContrasts(WT - LD, levels = design)
fit2 <- eBayes(contrasts.fit(fit, contr))
topTable(fit2)

contr <- makeContrasts(
  WT_vs_LD     = WT - LD,
  WT_vs_LD_NAC = WT - LD_NAC,
  LD_vs_LD_NAC = LD - LD_NAC ,
  WT_vs_WT_NAC = WT - WT_NAC,
  WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC = WT_NAC - LD_NAC,
  levels = design
)

library(limma)

fit <- lmFit(pb_mat, design)
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contr)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)


res_WT_vs_LD      <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_vs_LD",      n = Inf)
res_WT_vs_LD_NAC  <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_vs_LD_NAC",  n = Inf)
res_LD_vs_LD_NAC  <- topTable(fit2, coef = "LD_vs_LD_NAC",  n = Inf)
res_WT_vs_WT_NAC  <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_vs_WT_NAC",  n = Inf)
res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC", n = Inf)

write.csv(res_WT_vs_LD,      file.path(save.location, "no22_L23_limma_WT_vs_LD.csv"))
write.csv(res_WT_vs_LD_NAC,  file.path(save.location, "no22_L23_limma_WT_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))
write.csv(res_LD_vs_LD_NAC,  file.path(save.location, "no22_L23_limma_LD_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))
write.csv(res_WT_vs_WT_NAC,  file.path(save.location, "no22_L23_limma_WT_vs_WT_NAC.csv"))
write.csv(res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC, file.path(save.location, "no22_L23_limma_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))

library(ggplot2)

plot_volcano <- function(df, title) {
  df$Significant <- with(df, ifelse(adj.P.Val < 0.05 & abs(logFC) >= 0.25, "Yes", "No"))
  ggplot(df, aes(x = logFC, y = -log10(P.Value), color = Significant)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.25, 0.25), linetype = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "gray60", "Yes" = "red3")) +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)")
}

plot_volcano(res_WT_vs_LD, "WT vs LD (L2/3 PNs)")
#plot_volcano(res_LD_NAC_vs_LD, "LD_NAC vs LD (L2/3 PNs)")
#plot_volcano(res_WT_NAC_vs_WT, "WT_NAC vs WT (L2/3 PNs)")


```

```{r DE analysis with count aggregate in EdgeR}

library(edgeR)
DefaultAssay(l23) <- "Xenium"
l23 <- JoinLayers(l23)  # ensure one counts matrix

# build a samples dataframe: one row per cell
cell_meta <- l23@meta.data[, c("genotype","animal_id")]
cell_meta$cell <- rownames(cell_meta)

# aggregate counts by replicate (animal_id) within genotype
X <- GetAssayData(l23, slot = "counts")  # genes x cells
rep_keys <- paste(cell_meta$genotype, cell_meta$animal_id, sep = "__")

# sum across cells for each replicate
pb_mat <- rowsum(t(as.matrix(X)), group = rep_keys)  # (replicates x genes)
pb_mat <- t(pb_mat)                                 # (genes x replicates)

# build sample table
parts <- do.call(rbind, strsplit(colnames(pb_mat), "__"))
coldata <- data.frame(
  replicate = colnames(pb_mat),
  genotype  = factor(parts[,1], levels = c("WT","LD","WT_NAC","LD_NAC")),
  animal_id = parts[,2]
)

# edgeR
y <- DGEList(counts = pb_mat, samples = coldata)
y <- calcNormFactors(y, method = "TMM")

design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + genotype, data = y$samples)
colnames(design) <- levels(coldata$genotype)

y <- estimateDisp(y, design)
fit <- glmQLFit(y, design)

# contrasts you likely want
ct_WT_vs_LD      <- makeContrasts(LD - WT,      levels = design)
ct_WT_vs_LD_NAC  <- makeContrasts(LD_NAC - WT,  levels = design)
ct_LD_vs_LD_NAC  <- makeContrasts(LD_NAC - LD,  levels = design)
ct_WT_vs_WT_NAC  <- makeContrasts(WT_NAC - WT,  levels = design)
ct_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC  <- makeContrasts(LD_NAC - WT_NAC,  levels = design)

res_WT_vs_LD     <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_WT_vs_LD)
res_WT_vs_LD_NAC <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_WT_vs_LD_NAC)
res_LD_vs_LD_NAC <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_LD_vs_LD_NAC)
res_WT_vs_WT_NAC <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_WT_vs_WT_NAC)
res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC)

tab_WT_vs_LD     <- topTable <- topTags(res_WT_vs_LD, n = Inf)$table
tab_WT_vs_LD_NAC <- topTags(res_WT_vs_LD_NAC, n = Inf)$table
tab_LD_vs_LD_NAC <- topTags(res_LD_vs_LD_NAC, n = Inf)$table
tab_WT_vs_WT_NAC <- topTags(res_WT_vs_WT_NAC, n = Inf)$table
tab_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC <- topTags(res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC, n = Inf)$table

# add gene column and save
add_gene <- function(df) { df$gene <- rownames(df); df[, c("gene", colnames(df)[colnames(df)!="gene"])] }
tab_WT_vs_LD     <- add_gene(tab_WT_vs_LD)
tab_WT_vs_LD_NAC <- add_gene(tab_WT_vs_LD_NAC)
tab_LD_vs_LD_NAC <- add_gene(tab_LD_vs_LD_NAC)
tab_WT_vs_WT_NAC <- add_gene(tab_WT_vs_WT_NAC)
tab_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC <- add_gene(tab_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC)

write.csv(tab_WT_vs_LD,     file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_edgeR_WT_vs_LD.csv"),     row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(tab_WT_vs_LD_NAC,     file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_edgeR_WT_vs_LD_NAC.csv"),     row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(tab_LD_vs_LD_NAC, file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_edgeR_LD_vs_LD_NAC.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(tab_WT_vs_WT_NAC, file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_edgeR_WT_vs_WT_NAC.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(tab_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC,     file.path(save.location, "no22q_L23_edgeR_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC.csv"),     row.names = FALSE)


volcano <- function(df, lfc = "logFC", p = "PValue", gene = "gene", fdr_cut = 0.05, lfc_cut = 0.25, title = "") {
  # Calculate p-value cutoff that matches the FDR threshold
  p_cut <- calculate_pvalue_cutoff(df, p = p, fdr = "FDR", fdr_cut = fdr_cut)
  
  df$signif <- with(df, ifelse(FDR < fdr_cut & abs(get(lfc)) >= lfc_cut, "sig", "ns"))
  
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[lfc]], y = -log10(.data[[p]]), color = signif)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-lfc_cut, lfc_cut), linetype = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(ns = "grey70", sig = "red3")) +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 fold-change", y = "-log10(p)") +
    theme_classic()
  
  # Only add the horizontal line if a valid p_cut was found
  if (!is.na(p_cut) && is.finite(p_cut)) {
    p <- p + geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(p_cut), linetype = 2)
  } else {
    warning("Could not calculate p-value cutoff for FDR = ", fdr_cut, 
            ". No horizontal line drawn.")
  }
  
  return(p)
}

# Helper function to calculate p-value cutoff for a given FDR threshold
calculate_pvalue_cutoff <- function(df, p = "PValue", fdr = "FDR", fdr_cut = 0.05) {
  # Check if required columns exist
  if (!p %in% names(df) || !fdr %in% names(df)) {
    warning("Required columns not found in data frame")
    return(NA)
  }
  
  # Get p-values and FDR values
  pvals <- df[[p]]
  fdrs <- df[[fdr]]
  
  # Filter to significant hits at the given FDR cutoff
  sig_idx <- which(fdrs < fdr_cut)
  
  if (length(sig_idx) == 0) {
    # No significant hits, return NA
    return(NA)
  }
  
  # Find the maximum p-value among significant hits
  # This represents the p-value cutoff that would give us exactly these significant hits
  p_cut <- max(pvals[sig_idx], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  return(p_cut)
}

# Create a list of plots
plots <- list(
  volcano(tab_WT_vs_LD, title = "L2/3 PNs: WT vs LD"),
  volcano(tab_WT_vs_LD_NAC, title = "L2/3 PNs: WT vs LD_NAC"),
  volcano(tab_LD_vs_LD_NAC, title = "L2/3 PNs: LD vs LD_NAC"),
  volcano(tab_WT_vs_WT_NAC, title = "L2/3 PNs: WT vs WT_NAC"),
  volcano(tab_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC, title = "L2/3 PNs: WT_NAC vs LD_NAC")
)

# Save as multi-page PDF
ggsave("no22q_edgeR_volcano_plots.pdf", 
       plot = gridExtra::marrangeGrob(plots, nrow = 1, ncol = 1), 
       width = 8.5, height = 11, device = "pdf")
```

```{r heatmaps l23}
# Load required package
library(circlize)

# Create one PDF with all heatmaps (averaged by genotype)
pdf("all_heatmaps_by_genotype.pdf", width = 10, height = 12)

# List of gene sets
gene_sets <- list(
  "Oxidative Stress" = c("Foxo3", "Cat", "Sod1", "Sod2", "Sirt3", "Nfkb1", "Hif1a", 
                         "Gstp1", "Prdx1", "Prdx2", "Prdx5", "Hmox1", "Park7", 
                         "Psmb5", "Txnrd1", "Slc38a1", "Nfe2l2"),
  "Other Markers" = c("Nyap2", "Spock3", "Wif1", "Npas4", "Grm2", "Osgin1", "Srxn1", 
                      "Fos", "Vps13c", "Pclo"),
  "22q11 Genes" = c("Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt", "Tango2", "Mrpl40", 
                    "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8", "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh"),
  "mTOR Pathway" = c("Sesn2", "Ddit4", "Bhlhe40", "Mtor", "Tsc1", "Tsc2", "Uqcrc1", "Uqcrc2")
)

# Define genotype order: WT -> LD -> LD-NAC -> WT-NAC
genotype_order <- c("WT", "LD", "LD-NAC", "WT-NAC")

# Create each heatmap
for(i in 1:length(gene_sets)) {
  set_name <- names(gene_sets)[i]
  gene_list <- gene_sets[[i]]
  
  # Get genes that are actually in the dataset
  genes_found <- gene_list[gene_list %in% rownames(l23)]
  
  if(length(genes_found) > 0) {
    # Calculate average expression by genotype
    avg_expression <- AverageExpression(
      l23,
      features = genes_found,
      group.by = "genotype",
      assays = "SCT",
      slot = "data"
    )$SCT
    
    # Reorder the columns to match our preferred genotype order
    avg_expression <- avg_expression[, genotype_order]
    
    # Z-score normalization (row-wise)
    mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(avg_expression)))
    
    # Create color scale
    col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("navy", "white", "firebrick"))
    
    # Create heatmap
    hm <- Heatmap(
      mat_scaled,
      name = "Z-score",
      cluster_rows = TRUE,
      cluster_columns = FALSE,  # FALSE because we specified the order
      col = col_fun,
      row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
      column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
      heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Expression (z)"),
      column_title = paste(set_name, "(", length(genes_found), "genes )"),
      row_title = NULL,
      show_row_names = TRUE,
      show_column_names = TRUE
    )
    
    draw(hm)
    # Add page break except for last plot
    if(i < length(gene_sets)) {
      grid.newpage()
    }
    
    cat("Plotted", set_name, "with", length(genes_found), "genes\n")
  } else {
    cat("No genes found for:", set_name, "\n")
  }
}

dev.off()
cat("Saved genotype-averaged PDF: all_heatmaps_by_genotype.pdf\n")
```

```{r}
# Load required package
library(circlize)

# Create one PDF with all pairwise heatmaps
pdf("all_heatmaps_pairwise.pdf", width = 8, height = 10)

# List of gene sets
gene_sets <- list(
  "Oxidative Stress" = c("Foxo3", "Cat", "Sod1", "Sod2", "Sirt3", "Nfkb1", "Hif1a", 
                         "Gstp1", "Prdx1", "Prdx2", "Prdx5", "Hmox1", "Park7", 
                         "Psmb5", "Txnrd1", "Slc38a1", "Nfe2l2"),
  "Other Markers" = c("Nyap2", "Spock3", "Wif1", "Npas4", "Grm2", "Osgin1", "Srxn1", 
                       "Fos", "Vps13c", "Pclo"),
  "22q11 Genes" = c("Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt", "Tango2", "Mrpl40", 
                     "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8", "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh"),
  "mTOR Pathway" = c("Sesn2", "Ddit4", "Bhlhe40", "Mtor", "Tsc1", "Tsc2", "Uqcrc1", "Uqcrc2")
)

# Define pairwise comparisons
pairwise_comparisons <- list(
  "WT vs LD" = c("WT", "LD"),
  "WT vs LD-NAC" = c("WT", "LD-NAC"),
  "LD vs LD-NAC" = c("LD", "LD-NAC"),
  "WT vs WT-NAC" = c("WT", "WT-NAC")
)

# Create each heatmap for each gene set and each pairwise comparison
for(i in 1:length(gene_sets)) {
  set_name <- names(gene_sets)[i]
  gene_list <- gene_sets[[i]]
  
  # Get genes that are actually in the dataset
  genes_found <- gene_list[gene_list %in% rownames(l23)]
  
  if(length(genes_found) > 0) {
    # Calculate average expression by genotype for all genotypes first
    avg_expression_all <- AverageExpression(
      l23,
      features = genes_found,
      group.by = "genotype",
      assays = "SCT",
      slot = "data"
    )$SCT
    
    # Create heatmap for each pairwise comparison
    for(comp_name in names(pairwise_comparisons)) {
      genotypes <- pairwise_comparisons[[comp_name]]
      
      # Subset to the two genotypes for this comparison
      avg_expression_pair <- avg_expression_all[, genotypes, drop = FALSE]
      
      # Remove genes with zero variance (which cause NaN in scaling)
      row_variance <- apply(avg_expression_pair, 1, var)
      genes_with_variance <- names(row_variance)[row_variance > 0]
      
      if(length(genes_with_variance) > 0) {
        avg_expression_pair <- avg_expression_pair[genes_with_variance, , drop = FALSE]
        
        # Z-score normalization (row-wise)
        mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(avg_expression_pair)))
        
        # Create color scale
        col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("navy", "white", "firebrick"))
        
        # Create heatmap
        hm <- Heatmap(
          mat_scaled,
          name = "Z-score",
          cluster_rows = TRUE,
          cluster_columns = FALSE,  # Keep the order we specified
          col = col_fun,
          row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
          column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
          heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Expression (z)"),
          column_title = paste(set_name, "-", comp_name, "\n(", length(genes_with_variance), "genes )"),
          row_title = NULL,
          show_row_names = TRUE,
          show_column_names = TRUE
        )
        
        draw(hm)
        
        # Add page break except for the last plot of the last comparison
        if(!(i == length(gene_sets) && comp_name == names(pairwise_comparisons)[length(pairwise_comparisons)])) {
          grid.newpage()
        }
        
        cat("Plotted", set_name, "-", comp_name, "with", length(genes_with_variance), "genes (removed", 
            length(genes_found) - length(genes_with_variance), "zero-variance genes)\n")
      } else {
        cat("Skipped", set_name, "-", comp_name, ": no genes with variance after filtering\n")
        # Create an empty plot with message
        grid.text(paste("No variable genes found for", set_name, "-", comp_name),
                  gp = gpar(fontsize = 12))
        if(!(i == length(gene_sets) && comp_name == names(pairwise_comparisons)[length(pairwise_comparisons)])) {
          grid.newpage()
        }
      }
    }
  } else {
    cat("No genes found for:", set_name, "\n")
  }
}

dev.off()
cat("Saved pairwise comparison PDF: all_heatmaps_pairwise.pdf\n")

```

```{r}

# Comprehensive visualization of 22q11 gene expression

# 1. Violin plots for each gene
cat("Creating violin plots...\n")
vln_plots <- VlnPlot(l23,
                    features = available_22q11,
                    group.by = "genotype",
                    ncol = 4,
                    pt.size = 0,
                    raster = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("22q11 Genes Expression - L2/3 ") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

print(vln_plots)
ggsave("L23_22q11_genes_violin_plots.pdf", vln_plots, width = 16, height = 12)

# 2. Dot plot (shows expression level and percentage)
cat("Creating dot plot...\n")
dot_plot <- DotPlot(l23,
                   features = available_22q11,
                   group.by = "genotype",
                   cols = c("lightblue", "red"),
                   dot.scale = 6) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic")) +
  ggtitle("22q11 Genes - Expression Level and Percentage") +
  labs(x = "22q11 Genes", y = "Genotype")

print(dot_plot)
ggsave("L23_22q11_genes_dot_plot.pdf", dot_plot, width = 12, height = 8)

# 3. Heatmap of average expression
cat("Creating heatmap...\n")
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)

# Calculate average expression per genotype
avg_expression <- matrix(NA, nrow = length(available_22q11), ncol = length(unique(l23$genotype)))
rownames(avg_expression) <- available_22q11
colnames(avg_expression) <- unique(l23$genotype)

for (gene in available_22q11) {
  for (geno in unique(l23$genotype)) {
    cells <- WhichCells(l23, expression = genotype == geno)
    if (length(cells) > 0) {
      avg_expression[gene, geno] <- mean(GetAssayData(l23, slot = "data")[gene, cells])
    }
  }
}

# Convert to long format for ggplot
avg_exp_long <- melt(avg_expression)
colnames(avg_exp_long) <- c("Gene", "Genotype", "Expression")

# Create heatmap
heatmap_plot <- ggplot(avg_exp_long, aes(x = Genotype, y = Gene, fill = Expression)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red", 
                      midpoint = mean(avg_expression, na.rm = TRUE)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic")) +
  labs(title = "22q11 Genes - Average Expression Heatmap",
       x = "Genotype", y = "Gene") +
  coord_fixed(ratio = 0.8)

print(heatmap_plot)
ggsave("22q11_genes_heatmap.pdf", heatmap_plot, width = 10, height = 8)

# 4. Combined bar plot showing all comparisons
cat("Creating combined bar plot...\n")
library(tidyr)

# Prepare data for combined bar plot
combined_data <- data.frame(Gene = available_q22_genes)

for (comp_name in names(comparisons)) {
  for (gene in available_q22_genes) {
    if (gene %in% rownames(de_results[[comp_name]])) {
      combined_data[combined_data$Gene == gene, comp_name] <- de_results[[comp_name]][gene, "avg_log2FC"]
    } else {
      combined_data[combined_data$Gene == gene, comp_name] <- 0
    }
  }
}

# Convert to long format
combined_long <- pivot_longer(combined_data, 
                             cols = -Gene, 
                             names_to = "Comparison", 
                             values_to = "log2FC")

# Create combined bar plot
combined_bar <- ggplot(combined_long, aes(x = Gene, y = log2FC, fill = Comparison)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "22q11 Genes - Log2 Fold Change Across Comparisons",
       x = "Gene", y = "Log2 Fold Change") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(face = "italic"),
        legend.position = "bottom") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1")

print(combined_bar)
ggsave("22q11_genes_combined_barplot.pdf", combined_bar, width = 12, height = 8)

# 5. Expression UMAPs for key 22q11 genes
cat("Creating UMAP expression plots for key genes...\n")
key_genes <- c("Dgcr8", "Comt", "Ranbp1", "Hira")  # Most significant genes

for (gene in key_genes) {
  if (gene %in% available_q22_genes) {
    p <- FeaturePlot(l23_neurons, 
                    features = gene,
                    cols = c("lightgray", "red"),
                    raster = FALSE) +
      ggtitle(paste(gene, "- Expression in L2/3 Neurons"))
    print(p)
    ggsave(paste0("umap_", gene, "_L23_neurons.pdf"), p, width = 8, height = 6)
  }
}

# 6. Summary statistics table
cat("\n=== Expression Summary Statistics ===\n")
summary_stats <- data.frame(
  Gene = available_q22_genes,
  Detection_Rate = NA,
  Mean_Expression_WT = NA,
  Mean_Expression_LD = NA,
  Fold_Change_LD_vs_WT = NA,
  P_Value_LD_vs_WT = NA
)

for (gene in available_q22_genes) {
  # Detection rate
  detection_rate <- mean(GetAssayData(l23_neurons, slot = "counts")[gene, ] > 0)
  summary_stats$Detection_Rate[summary_stats$Gene == gene] <- round(detection_rate * 100, 1)
  
  # Mean expression
  wt_cells <- WhichCells(l23_neurons, expression = genotype == "WT")
```
