```{r}
  ld_cells <- WhichCells(l23_neurons, expression = genotype == "LD")
  
  summary_stats$Mean_Expression_WT[summary_stats$Gene == gene] <- 
    round(mean(GetAssayData(l23_neurons, slot = "data")[gene, wt_cells]), 3)
  summary_stats$Mean_Expression_LD[summary_stats$Gene == gene] <- 
    round(mean(GetAssayData(l23_neurons, slot = "data")[gene, ld_cells]), 3)
  
  # DE results
  if (gene %in% rownames(de_results$LD_vs_WT)) {
    summary_stats$Fold_Change_LD_vs_WT[summary_stats$Gene == gene] <- 
      round(2^de_results$LD_vs_WT[gene, "avg_log2FC"], 3)
    summary_stats$P_Value_LD_vs_WT[summary_stats$Gene == gene] <- 
      formatC(de_results$LD_vs_WT[gene, "p_val_adj"], format = "e", digits = 2)
  }
}

print("Summary Statistics:")
print(summary_stats)
write.csv(summary_stats, "22q11_genes_summary_statistics.csv", row.names = FALSE)

cat("\nAll visualizations completed and saved!\n")
cat("Created:\n")
cat("- Violin plots: 22q11_genes_violin_plots.pdf\n")
cat("- Dot plot: 22q11_genes_dot_plot.pdf\n")
cat("- Heatmap: 22q11_genes_heatmap.pdf\n")
cat("- Combined bar plot: 22q11_genes_combined_barplot.pdf\n")
cat("- UMAP expression plots for key genes\n")
cat("- Summary statistics: 22q11_genes_summary_statistics.csv\n")


```

```{r creat heatmaps for l23 using genelists}
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(circlize)
avg_expr_by_genotype <- AverageExpression(
  l23,
  assays = "SCT",
  group.by = "genotype"
)
avg_expr_by_genotype$SCT[1:10, ]



# Function to create and save heatmap for a gene list
create_heatmap_pdf <- function(gene_list, filename, title = "") {
  # Subset matrix to genes of interest
  mat_sub <- mat[rownames(mat) %in% gene_list, ]
  
  # Remove any genes not found
  genes_found <- gene_list[gene_list %in% rownames(mat)]
  if(length(genes_found) == 0) {
    cat("No genes found for:", title, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  mat_sub <- mat_sub[genes_found, , drop = FALSE]
  
  # Z-score normalization
  mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat_sub)))
  
  # Create color scale
  col_fun <- colorRamp2(c(-2, 0, 2), c("navy", "white", "firebrick"))
  
  # Create heatmap
  hm <- Heatmap(
    mat_scaled,
    name = "Z-score",
    cluster_rows = TRUE,
    cluster_columns = FALSE,
    col = col_fun,
    row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
    column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 10),
    heatmap_legend_param = list(title = "Expression (z)"),
    column_title = title,
    row_title = paste(length(genes_found), "genes")
  )
  
  # Save as PDF
  pdf(file = filename, width = 8, height = 10)
  draw(hm)
  dev.off()
  
  cat("Saved:", filename, "with", length(genes_found), "genes\n")
}

# Create and save heatmaps for all gene lists

# 1. Oxidative stress genes
create_heatmap_pdf(
  gene_list = c("Foxo3", "Cat", "Sod1", "Sod2", "Sirt3", "Nfkb1", "Hif1a", "Gstp1", 
                "Prdx1", "Prdx2", "Prdx5", "Hmox1", "Park7", "Psmb5", "Txnrd1", 
                "Slc38a1", "Nfe2l2"),
  filename = "heatmap_oxidative_stress.pdf",
  title = "Oxidative Stress Genes"
)

# 2. Other markers
create_heatmap_pdf(
  gene_list = c("Nyap2", "Spock3", "Wif1", "Npas4", "Grm2", "Osgin1", "Srxn1", 
                "Fos", "Trmt2a", "Prodh", "Txnrd2"),
  filename = "heatmap_other_markers.pdf",
  title = "Other Markers"
)

# 3. 22q11 genes
create_heatmap_pdf(
  gene_list = c("Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt", "Tango2", "Mrpl40", 
                "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8", "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh"),
  filename = "heatmap_22q11_genes.pdf",
  title = "22q11 Genes"
)

# 4. mTOR pathway genes
create_heatmap_pdf(
  gene_list = c("Sesn2", "Ddit4", "Bhlhe40", "Mtor", "Tsc1", "Tsc2", "Uqcrc1", "Uqcrc2"),
  filename = "heatmap_mTOR_pathway.pdf",
  title = "mTOR Pathway Genes"
)

```
```{r Violin plots of gene lists}
genes_22q11 <- c(
  "Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt", "Tango2", "Mrpl40", "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8", "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh")

genes_mito_metablism <-  c("Sesn2", "Ddit4", "Bhlhe40", "Mtor", "Tsc1", "Tsc2", "Uqcrc1", "Uqcrc2")

genes_annotated_mito <- c("Foxo3", "Cat", "Sod1", "Sod2", "Sirt3", "Nfkb1", "Hif1a", "Gstp1", "Prdx1", "Prdx2", "Prdx5", "Hmox1", "Park7", "Psmb5", "Txnrd1", "Slc38a1", "Nfe2l2")

DefaultAssay(l23) <- "SCT"

# Create violin plots for genes_22q11
violin_22q11 <- VlnPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_22q11, rownames(l23)), group.by = "genotype", pt.size = 0) +
  ggtitle("22q11 genes in L2/3 PNs by genotype") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  NoLegend()

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_l23_violin_22q11.pdf", violin_22q11, width = 12, height = 8)

# by subclusters within L2/3
violin_22q11_subclusters <- VlnPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_22q11, rownames(l23)), group.by = "seurat_clusters", pt.size = 0) +
  ggtitle("22q11 genes across L2/3 PN subclusters") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  NoLegend()

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_l23_violin_22q11_subclusters.pdf", violin_22q11_subclusters, width = 12, height = 8)

# Create violin plots for genes_mito_metablism
violin_mito_metabolism <- VlnPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_mito_metablism, rownames(l23)), group.by = "genotype", pt.size = 0) +
  ggtitle("Mitochondrial metabolism genes in L2/3 PNs by genotype") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  NoLegend()

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_l23_violin_mito_metabolism.pdf", violin_mito_metabolism, width = 12, height = 8)

# by subclusters within L2/3
violin_mito_metabolism_subclusters <- VlnPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_mito_metablism, rownames(l23)), group.by = "seurat_clusters", pt.size = 0) +
  ggtitle("Mitochondrial metabolism genes across L2/3 PN subclusters") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  NoLegend()

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_l23_violin_mito_metabolism_subclusters.pdf", violin_mito_metabolism_subclusters, width = 12, height = 8)

# Create violin plots for genes_annotated_mito
violin_annotated_mito <- VlnPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_annotated_mito, rownames(l23)), group.by = "genotype", pt.size = 0) +
  ggtitle("Annotated mitochondrial genes in L2/3 PNs by genotype") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  NoLegend()

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_l23_violin_annotated_mito.pdf", violin_annotated_mito, width = 12, height = 8)

# by subclusters within L2/3
violin_annotated_mito_subclusters <- VlnPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_annotated_mito, rownames(l23)), group.by = "seurat_clusters", pt.size = 0) +
  ggtitle("Annotated mitochondrial genes across L2/3 PN subclusters") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  NoLegend()

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_l23_violin_annotated_mito_subclusters.pdf", violin_annotated_mito_subclusters, width = 12, height = 8)



```
```{r Dotplots of gene lists}

genes_22q11 <- c(
  "Ranbp1", "Dgcr8", "Cdc45", "Trmt2a", "Comt", "Tango2", "Mrpl40", "Slc25a1", "Zdhhc8", "Txnrd2", "Hira", "Prodh")

genes_mito_metablism <-  c("Sesn2", "Ddit4", "Bhlhe40", "Mtor", "Tsc1", "Tsc2", "Uqcrc1", "Uqcrc2")

genes_annotated_mito <- c("Foxo3", "Cat", "Sod1", "Sod2", "Sirt3", "Nfkb1", "Hif1a", "Gstp1", "Prdx1", "Prdx2", "Prdx5", "Hmox1", "Park7", "Psmb5", "Txnrd1", "Slc38a1", "Nfe2l2")

DefaultAssay(l23) <- "SCT"

# Create the dot plot with customizations
dotplot_22q11 <- DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_22q11, rownames(l23)), group.by = "genotype") +
  ggtitle("22q11 genes in L2/3 PNs by genotype") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red")
# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_dotplot_22q11.pdf", dotplot_22q11, width = 12, height = 8)
# by subclusters within L2/3
dotplot_subclusters <- DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_22q11, rownames(l23)), group.by = "seurat_clusters") +
  ggtitle("22q11 genes across L2/3 PN subclusters") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red")
# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_dotplot_22q11_subclusters.pdf", dotplot_subclusters, width = 12, height = 8)



# For genes_mito_metablism
dotplot_mito_metabolism <- DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_mito_metablism, rownames(l23)), group.by = "genotype") +
  ggtitle("Mitochondrial metabolism genes in L2/3 PNs by genotype") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red")

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_dotplot_mito_metabolism.pdf", dotplot_mito_metabolism, width = 12, height = 8)

# by subclusters within L2/3
dotplot_mito_metabolism_subclusters <- DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_mito_metablism, rownames(l23)), group.by = "seurat_clusters") +
  ggtitle("Mitochondrial metabolism genes across L2/3 PN subclusters") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red")

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_dotplot_mito_metabolism_subclusters.pdf", dotplot_mito_metabolism_subclusters, width = 12, height = 8)

# For genes_annotated_mito
dotplot_annotated_mito <- DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_annotated_mito, rownames(l23)), group.by = "genotype") +
  ggtitle("Annotated mitochondrial genes in L2/3 PNs by genotype") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red")

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_dotplot_annotated_mito.pdf", dotplot_annotated_mito, width = 12, height = 8)

# by subclusters within L2/3
dotplot_annotated_mito_subclusters <- DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_annotated_mito, rownames(l23)), group.by = "seurat_clusters") +
  ggtitle("Annotated mitochondrial genes across L2/3 PN subclusters") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_color_gradient2(low = "blue", mid = "white", high = "red")

# Save with appropriate size
ggsave("no22q_dotplot_annotated_mito_subclusters.pdf", dotplot_annotated_mito_subclusters, width = 12, height = 8)




```
```{r all_samples cell proportion analysis - ANOVA with Dunnett Post-hoc}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(multcomp)
library(purrr)

# 0) choose object + cluster column (works for all_samples or l23)
obj <- all_samples                    # or l23
cluster_col <- "seurat_clusters"      # or your subcluster column

# 1) per-animal proportions per cluster
prop_per_animal <- obj@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(genotype, animal_id, !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(cluster = !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 2) mean±SE per genotype (for plotting/reporting)
grp_summary <- prop_per_animal %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_prop = mean(prop),
    se = sd(prop) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
    n_animals = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3) ANOVA + Dunnett (WT as reference) per cluster
run_one <- function(df, ref = "WT") {
  df$genotype <- stats::relevel(factor(df$genotype), ref = ref)
  fit <- aov(prop ~ genotype, data = df)
  anova_p <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

  dun <- multcomp::glht(fit, linfct = mcp(genotype = "Dunnett"))
  ds <- summary(dun)

  tibble::tibble(
    contrast = names(ds$test$coefficients),
    estimate = as.numeric(ds$test$coefficients),   # difference vs ref (WT)
    t        = as.numeric(ds$test$tstat),
    p_adj    = as.numeric(ds$test$pvalues),
    anova_p  = anova_p
  )
}

all_clusters <- sort(unique(prop_per_animal$cluster))

dunnett_by_cluster <- purrr::map_dfr(
  all_clusters,
  ~ run_one(dplyr::filter(prop_per_animal, cluster == .x), ref = "WT") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(cluster = .x),
  .id = NULL
) %>%
  dplyr::relocate(cluster, .before = contrast) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(contrast) %>%                      # BH adjust across clusters per contrast
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj_BH_across_clusters = p.adjust(p_adj, method = "BH")) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

## ----- 4) Quick visuals + Save as PDF -----

# (a) Heatmap of mean proportions (%)
heat_df <- grp_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(pct = 100 * mean_prop)

pdf(file.path(save.location, "Cluster_Proportion_Heatmap.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = factor(cluster), y = genotype, fill = pct)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Genotype",
    fill = "% Cells",
    title = "Proportion per Cluster (Mean Across Animals)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
dev.off()


# (b) Faceted bars with mean ± SE
pdf(file.path(save.location, "Cluster_Proportion_FacetedBars.pdf"), width = 10, height = 7)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_prop)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_prop - se, ymax = mean_prop + se), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proportion of Cells",
    title = "Cluster Proportions by Genotype (Mean ± SE)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
dev.off()


# (c) Stacked column plot: cluster composition by genotype
grp_summary$cluster <- factor(grp_summary$cluster, levels = sort(unique(grp_summary$cluster)))
pal <- Seurat::DiscretePalette(length(levels(grp_summary$cluster)), palette = "polychrome")
names(pal) <- levels(grp_summary$cluster)

pdf(file.path(save.location, "Cluster_Composition_StackedBars.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_prop, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75, position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = "Cluster") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Cluster Composition by Genotype (Mean Across Animals)",
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Composition (%)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
dev.off()

top_hits <- dunnett_by_cluster %>%
  filter(p_adj_BH_across_clusters < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(p_adj_BH_across_clusters)

ggplot(top_hits, aes(x=factor(cluster), y=estimate, fill=contrast)) +
  geom_col(position="dodge") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="gray40") +
  labs(y="Δ proportion (vs WT)", x="cluster",
       title="Clusters with significant proportion changes vs WT") +
  theme_classic()

# 5) save tables if you want
write.csv(grp_summary, file.path(save.location, "cluster_props_mean_SE_by_genotype.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dunnett_by_cluster, file.path(save.location, "cluster_prop_Dunnett_vs_WT_by_cluster.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
```
```{r l23 cell proportion analysis - ANOVA with Dunnett Post-hoc}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(multcomp)
library(purrr)

# 0) choose object + cluster column (works for all_samples or l23)
obj <- l23                    # or l23
cluster_col <- "seurat_clusters"      # or your subcluster column

# 1) per-animal proportions per cluster
prop_per_animal <- obj@meta.data %>%
  dplyr::select(genotype, animal_id, !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(cluster = !!sym(cluster_col)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 2) mean±SE per genotype (for plotting/reporting)
grp_summary <- prop_per_animal %>%
  dplyr::group_by(genotype, cluster) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_prop = mean(prop),
    se = sd(prop) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),
    n_animals = dplyr::n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# 3) ANOVA + Dunnett (WT as reference) per cluster
run_one <- function(df, ref = "WT") {
  df$genotype <- stats::relevel(factor(df$genotype), ref = ref)
  fit <- aov(prop ~ genotype, data = df)
  anova_p <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]

  dun <- multcomp::glht(fit, linfct = mcp(genotype = "Dunnett"))
  ds <- summary(dun)

  tibble::tibble(
    contrast = names(ds$test$coefficients),
    estimate = as.numeric(ds$test$coefficients),   # difference vs ref (WT)
    t        = as.numeric(ds$test$tstat),
    p_adj    = as.numeric(ds$test$pvalues),
    anova_p  = anova_p
  )
}

all_clusters <- sort(unique(prop_per_animal$cluster))

dunnett_by_cluster <- purrr::map_dfr(
  all_clusters,
  ~ run_one(dplyr::filter(prop_per_animal, cluster == .x), ref = "WT") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(cluster = .x),
  .id = NULL
) %>%
  dplyr::relocate(cluster, .before = contrast) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(contrast) %>%                      # BH adjust across clusters per contrast
  dplyr::mutate(p_adj_BH_across_clusters = p.adjust(p_adj, method = "BH")) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

## ----- 4) Quick visuals + Save as PDF -----

# (a) Heatmap of mean proportions (%)
heat_df <- grp_summary %>%
  dplyr::mutate(pct = 100 * mean_prop)

pdf(file.path(save.location, "l23_Cluster_Proportion_Heatmap.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
ggplot(heat_df, aes(x = factor(cluster), y = genotype, fill = pct)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(
    x = "Cluster",
    y = "Genotype",
    fill = "% Cells",
    title = "l23_Proportion per Cluster (Mean Across Animals)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)
dev.off()


# (b) Faceted bars with mean ± SE
pdf(file.path(save.location, "l23_Cluster_Proportion_FacetedBars.pdf"), width = 10, height = 7)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_prop)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.9) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_prop - se, ymax = mean_prop + se), width = 0.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ cluster, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Proportion of Cells",
    title = "l23_Cluster Proportions by Genotype (Mean ± SE)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)
dev.off()


# (c) Stacked column plot: cluster composition by genotype
grp_summary$cluster <- factor(grp_summary$cluster, levels = sort(unique(grp_summary$cluster)))
pal <- Seurat::DiscretePalette(length(levels(grp_summary$cluster)), palette = "polychrome")
names(pal) <- levels(grp_summary$cluster)

pdf(file.path(save.location, "l23_Cluster_Composition_StackedBars.pdf"), width = 8, height = 6)
ggplot(grp_summary, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_prop, fill = cluster)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.75, position = "stack") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = "Cluster") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "l23_Cluster Composition by Genotype (Mean Across Animals)",
    x = "Genotype",
    y = "Composition (%)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
dev.off()

top_hits <- dunnett_by_cluster %>%
  filter(p_adj_BH_across_clusters < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(p_adj_BH_across_clusters)

ggplot(top_hits, aes(x=factor(cluster), y=estimate, fill=contrast)) +
  geom_col(position="dodge") +
  geom_hline(yintercept=0, color="gray40") +
  labs(y="Δ proportion (vs WT)", x="cluster",
       title="Clusters with significant proportion changes vs WT") +
  theme_classic()

# 5) save tables if you want
write.csv(grp_summary, file.path(save.location, "l23_cluster_props_mean_SE_by_genotype.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(dunnett_by_cluster, file.path(save.location, "l23_cluster_prop_Dunnett_vs_WT_by_cluster.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r l23}

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(ggrepel)

create_volcano <- function(de_results, title, p_cutoff = 0.05, fc_cutoff = 0.25) {
  # Prepare data
  volcano_data <- de_results %>%
    mutate(
      gene = rownames(.),
      log10_pval = -log10(p_val_adj),
      significance = case_when(
        p_val_adj < p_cutoff & avg_log2FC > fc_cutoff ~ "Up",
        p_val_adj < p_cutoff & avg_log2FC < -fc_cutoff ~ "Down", 
        TRUE ~ "Not significant"
      )
    )
  
  # Get top genes for labeling
  top_genes <- volcano_data %>%
    filter(significance != "Not significant") %>%
    arrange(p_val_adj) %>%
    head(10)
  
  # Create plot
  p <- ggplot(volcano_data, aes(x = avg_log2FC, y = log10_pval, color = significance)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(p_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-fc_cutoff, fc_cutoff), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = top_genes,
      aes(label = gene),
      size = 3,
      max.overlaps = 20,
      box.padding = 0.5,
      segment.color = "gray50"
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "Up" = "#E31A1C",
        "Down" = "#1F78B4", 
        "Not significant" = "gray70"
      )
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title,
      x = "Log2 Fold Change",
      y = "-Log10 Adjusted P-value"
    ) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 10),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 9),
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(p)
}


# Create and save each volcano plot
p1 <- create_volcano(de_wt_vs_ld, "LD vs WT in L2/3")
p2 <- create_volcano(de_wt_vs_ldnac, "LD_NAC vs WT in L2/3")
p3 <- create_volcano(de_ld_vs_ldnac, "LD_NAC vs LD in L2/3")
p4 <- create_volcano(de_wt_vs_wtnac, "WT_NAC vs WT in L2/3")
p5 <- create_volcano(de_wtnac_vs_ldnac, "LD_NAC vs WT_NAC in L2/3")

# Save individual plots
ggsave("v2_volcano_LD_vs_WT.pdf", p1, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("v2_volcano_LDNAC_vs_WT.pdf", p2, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("v2_volcano_LDNAC_vs_LD.pdf", p3, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("v2_volcano_WTNAC_vs_WT.pdf", p4, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
ggsave("v2_volcano_LDNAC_vs_WTNAC.pdf", p5, width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```
```{r l23}


library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

gene <- "Sod1"  # change if needed

# make sure we're using Xenium counts for a RNAscope-like metric
DefaultAssay(l23) <- "Xenium"
l23 <- JoinLayers(l23)
# Set identities to genotype for DE analysis
Idents(l23) <- "genotype"
# check gene exists
if (!gene %in% rownames(l23)) stop(paste("Gene not in panel:", gene))

# pull per-cell expression + metadata
df <- data.frame(
  expr = as.numeric(GetAssayData(l23, slot = "counts")[gene, colnames(l23)]),
  animal_id = l23$animal_id,
  genotype  = l23$genotype
)

# per-animal means
rep_means <- df %>%
  group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  summarize(mean_expr = mean(expr, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

# group summary for plotting
grp_sum <- rep_means %>%
  group_by(genotype) %>%
  summarize(
    mean = mean(mean_expr),
    se   = sd(mean_expr) / sqrt(n()),
    n    = n(), .groups = "drop"
  )

# ANOVA + Tukey
fit <- aov(mean_expr ~ genotype, data = rep_means)
anova_res <- summary(fit)
tukey_res <- TukeyHSD(fit)

print(anova_res)
print(tukey_res)

ggplot(rep_means, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_expr)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se),
    width = 0.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean),
    size = 3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = paste(gene, "per-animal mean (L2/3 PNs)"),
    y = "mean counts per cell",
    x = "genotype"
  ) +
  theme_classic()


```
```{r l23}

thr <- 0  # >0 counts = positive; tweak if you want a higher puncta threshold

rep_pos <- df %>%
  group_by(genotype, animal_id) %>%
  summarize(pct_pos = mean(expr > thr) * 100, .groups = "drop")

# ANOVA + Tukey on % positive
fit_pos <- aov(pct_pos ~ genotype, data = rep_pos)
print(summary(fit_pos))
print(TukeyHSD(fit_pos))

# plot
grp_sum_pos <- rep_pos %>%
  group_by(genotype) %>%
  summarize(mean = mean(pct_pos), se = sd(pct_pos)/sqrt(n()), n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(rep_means, aes(x = genotype, y = mean_expr)) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.12, height = 0, size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean, ymin = mean - se, ymax = mean + se),
    width = 0.2,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = grp_sum,
    aes(x = genotype, y = mean),
    size = 3,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = paste(gene, "per-animal mean (L2/3 PNs)"),
    y = "mean counts per cell",
    x = "genotype"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

```
```{r l23}

pb <- AggregateExpression(
  l23,
  assays = "Xenium",
  group.by = c("genotype", "animal_id"),
  slot = "counts"
)
counts <- pb$Xenium

meta <- data.frame(
  genotype  = sub("_.*", "", colnames(counts)),
  animal_id = sub("^[^_]*_", "", colnames(counts))
)
meta$genotype  <- gsub("-", "_", meta$genotype)
meta$animal_id <- gsub("-", "_", meta$animal_id)


meta$genotype <- factor(meta$genotype, levels = c("WT", "LD", "LD_NAC", "WT_NAC"))

library(DESeq2)

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = round(counts),
  colData = meta,
  design = ~ genotype
)

dds <- DESeq(dds)

resultsNames(dds)

res_LD_vs_WT      <- results(dds, name = "genotype_LD_vs_WT")
res_LD_NAC_vs_WT  <- results(dds, name = "genotype_LD_NAC_vs_WT")
res_WT_NAC_vs_WT  <- results(dds, name = "genotype_WT_NAC_vs_WT")

# for non-reference contrasts (optional):
res_LD_NAC_vs_LD  <- results(dds, contrast = c("genotype", "LD_NAC", "LD"))
res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC <- results(dds, contrast = c("genotype", "WT_NAC", "LD_NAC"))


write.csv(as.data.frame(res_LD_vs_WT),      file.path(save.location, "L23_DESeq2_LD_vs_WT.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_LD_NAC_vs_WT),  file.path(save.location, "L23_DESeq2_LD_NAC_vs_WT.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_WT_NAC_vs_WT),  file.path(save.location, "L23_DESeq2_WT_NAC_vs_WT.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_LD_NAC_vs_LD),  file.path(save.location, "L23_DESeq2_LD_NAC_vs_LD.csv"))
write.csv(as.data.frame(res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC), file.path(save.location, "L23_DESeq2_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))

library(ggplot2)
volcano <- function(df, title) {
  df$Signif <- ifelse(df$padj < 0.05 & abs(df$log2FoldChange) > 0.25, "Yes", "No")
  ggplot(df, aes(x = log2FoldChange, y = -log10(pvalue), color = Signif)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.25, 0.25), linetype = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "gray70", "Yes" = "red3")) +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)") +
    theme_classic()
}

volcano(res_LD_vs_WT, "L2/3 PNs: LD vs WT")
volcano(res_LD_NAC_vs_WT, "L2/3 PNs: LD_NAC vs WT")
volcano(res_WT_NAC_vs_WT, "L2/3 PNs: WT_NAC vs WT")


```
```{r l23}

pb <- AggregateExpression(
  l23,
  assays = "SCT",
  group.by = c("genotype", "animal_id"),  # aggregate per replicate within each genotype
  slot = "data"   # or "counts" for raw sums
)




pb$SCT
meta <- as.data.frame(do.call(rbind, strsplit(colnames(pb_mat), "_")))
colnames(meta) <- c("genotype", "animal_id")

meta$genotype <- gsub("-", "_", meta$genotype)
meta$genotype <- factor(meta$genotype)

design <- model.matrix(~0 + genotype, data = meta)
colnames(design) <- levels(meta$genotype)
colnames(design)


fit <- lmFit(pb_mat, design)
contr <- makeContrasts(WT - LD, levels = design)
fit2 <- eBayes(contrasts.fit(fit, contr))
topTable(fit2)

contr <- makeContrasts(
  WT_vs_LD     = WT - LD,
  WT_vs_LD_NAC = WT - LD_NAC,
  LD_vs_LD_NAC = LD - LD_NAC ,
  WT_vs_WT_NAC = WT - WT_NAC,
  WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC = WT_NAC - LD_NAC,
  levels = design
)

library(limma)

fit <- lmFit(pb_mat, design)
fit2 <- contrasts.fit(fit, contr)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit2)


res_WT_vs_LD      <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_vs_LD",      n = Inf)
res_WT_vs_LD_NAC  <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_vs_LD_NAC",  n = Inf)
res_LD_vs_LD_NAC  <- topTable(fit2, coef = "LD_vs_LD_NAC",  n = Inf)
res_WT_vs_WT_NAC  <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_vs_WT_NAC",  n = Inf)
res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC <- topTable(fit2, coef = "WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC", n = Inf)

write.csv(res_WT_vs_LD,      file.path(save.location, "L23_limma_WT_vs_LD.csv"))
write.csv(res_WT_vs_LD_NAC,  file.path(save.location, "L23_limma_WT_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))
write.csv(res_LD_vs_LD_NAC,  file.path(save.location, "L23_limma_LD_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))
write.csv(res_WT_vs_WT_NAC,  file.path(save.location, "L23_limma_WT_vs_WT_NAC.csv"))
write.csv(res_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC, file.path(save.location, "L23_limma_WT_NAC_vs_LD_NAC.csv"))

library(ggplot2)

plot_volcano <- function(df, title) {
  df$Significant <- with(df, ifelse(adj.P.Val < 0.05 & abs(logFC) >= 0.25, "Yes", "No"))
  ggplot(df, aes(x = logFC, y = -log10(P.Value), color = Significant)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-0.25, 0.25), linetype = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("No" = "gray60", "Yes" = "red3")) +
    theme_classic() +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 Fold Change", y = "-log10(p-value)")
}

plot_volcano(res_WT_vs_LD, "WT vs LD (L2/3 PNs)")
#plot_volcano(res_LD_NAC_vs_LD, "LD_NAC vs LD (L2/3 PNs)")
#plot_volcano(res_WT_NAC_vs_WT, "WT_NAC vs WT (L2/3 PNs)")


```
```{r l23 edgeR}

library(edgeR)
DefaultAssay(l23) <- "Xenium"
l23 <- JoinLayers(l23)  # ensure one counts matrix

# build a samples dataframe: one row per cell
cell_meta <- l23@meta.data[, c("genotype","animal_id")]
cell_meta$cell <- rownames(cell_meta)

# aggregate counts by replicate (animal_id) within genotype
X <- GetAssayData(l23, slot = "counts")  # genes x cells
rep_keys <- paste(cell_meta$genotype, cell_meta$animal_id, sep = "__")

# sum across cells for each replicate
pb_mat <- rowsum(t(as.matrix(X)), group = rep_keys)  # (replicates x genes)
pb_mat <- t(pb_mat)                                 # (genes x replicates)

# build sample table
parts <- do.call(rbind, strsplit(colnames(pb_mat), "__"))
coldata <- data.frame(
  replicate = colnames(pb_mat),
  genotype  = factor(parts[,1], levels = c("WT","LD","WT_NAC","LD_NAC")),
  animal_id = parts[,2]
)

# edgeR
y <- DGEList(counts = pb_mat, samples = coldata)
y <- calcNormFactors(y, method = "TMM")

design <- model.matrix(~ 0 + genotype, data = y$samples)
colnames(design) <- levels(coldata$genotype)

y <- estimateDisp(y, design)
fit <- glmQLFit(y, design)

# contrasts you likely want
ct_WT_vs_LD      <- makeContrasts(WT - LD,      levels = design)
ct_LD_NAC_vs_LD  <- makeContrasts(LD_NAC - LD,  levels = design)
ct_WT_NAC_vs_WT  <- makeContrasts(WT_NAC - WT,  levels = design)

res_WT_vs_LD     <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_WT_vs_LD)
res_LD_NAC_vs_LD <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_LD_NAC_vs_LD)
res_WT_NAC_vs_WT <- glmQLFTest(fit, contrast = ct_WT_NAC_vs_WT)

tab_WT_vs_LD     <- topTable <- topTags(res_WT_vs_LD,     n = Inf)$table
tab_LD_NAC_vs_LD <- topTags(res_LD_NAC_vs_LD, n = Inf)$table
tab_WT_NAC_vs_WT <- topTags(res_WT_NAC_vs_WT, n = Inf)$table

# add gene column and save
add_gene <- function(df) { df$gene <- rownames(df); df[, c("gene", colnames(df)[colnames(df)!="gene"])] }
tab_WT_vs_LD     <- add_gene(tab_WT_vs_LD)
tab_LD_NAC_vs_LD <- add_gene(tab_LD_NAC_vs_LD)
tab_WT_NAC_vs_WT <- add_gene(tab_WT_NAC_vs_WT)

write.csv(tab_WT_vs_LD,     file.path(save.location, "L23_edgeR_WT_vs_LD.csv"),     row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(tab_LD_NAC_vs_LD, file.path(save.location, "L23_edgeR_LD_NAC_vs_LD.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(tab_WT_NAC_vs_WT, file.path(save.location, "L23_edgeR_WT_NAC_vs_WT.csv"), row.names = FALSE)










library(ggplot2)

volcano <- function(df, lfc = "logFC", p = "PValue", gene = "gene", fdr_cut = 0.05, lfc_cut = 0.25, title = "") {
  df$signif <- with(df, ifelse(FDR < fdr_cut & abs(get(lfc)) >= lfc_cut, "sig", "ns"))
  ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[lfc]], y = -log10(.data[[p]]), color = signif)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(-lfc_cut, lfc_cut), linetype = 2) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(fdr_cut), linetype = 2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(ns = "grey70", sig = "red3")) +
    labs(title = title, x = "log2 fold-change", y = "-log10(p)") +
    theme_classic()
}

volcano(tab_WT_vs_LD,     title = "L2/3 PNs: WT vs LD")
volcano(tab_LD_NAC_vs_LD, title = "L2/3 PNs: LD_NAC vs LD")
volcano(tab_WT_NAC_vs_WT, title = "L2/3 PNs: WT_NAC vs WT")





genes_22q11 <- c(
  "Ranbp1",
"Dgcr8",
"Cdc45",
"Trmt2a",
"Comt",
"Tango2",
"Mrpl40",
"Slc25a1",
"Zdhhc8",
"Txnrd2",
"Hira",
"Prodh"
 # adjust to your exact panel subset
)

DefaultAssay(l23) <- "SCT"
DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_22q11, rownames(l23)), group.by = "genotype") +
  ggtitle("22q11 genes in L2/3 PNs by genotype")

# by subclusters within L2/3
DotPlot(l23, features = intersect(genes_22q11, rownames(l23)), group.by = "seurat_clusters") +
  ggtitle("22q11 genes across L2/3 PN subclusters")

# install if needed: remotes::install_github("sebastian-luna-valero/msigdbr")
library(msigdbr)
library(fgsea)

# ranks from a DE table, e.g., LD_NAC vs LD
ranks <- tab_LD_NAC_vs_LD$logFC
names(ranks) <- tab_LD_NAC_vs_LD$gene
ranks <- sort(ranks, decreasing = TRUE)

# get mouse Hallmark or GO sets
msig <- msigdbr(species = "Mus musculus", category = "H")
pathways <- split(msig$gene_symbol, msig$gs_name)

# run fgsea (restrict to genes present)
fg <- fgsea(pathways = pathways, stats = ranks, nperm = 5000)
fg <- fg[order(fg$padj), ]
head(fg[, c("pathway","NES","padj")])

```

```{r}

# If you want to keep clusters as factor but still assign cell types
all_samples$cell_type <- NA

# Manual assignment (safe approach) - UPDATED based on marker gene analysis
cluster_mapping <- data.frame(
  cluster = 0:12,
  cell_type = c(
    "Excitatory Neurons (L2-3)",
    "GABAergic Interneurons (Drd1/Drd2+)", 
    "Excitatory Neurons (L4)",
    "Excitatory Neurons (L5)",
    "GABAergic Interneurons (Multiple)",
    "Activated/Reactive Cells",
    "Endothelial Cells",
    "Astrocytes",
    "Oligodendrocyte Precursor Cells",
    "GABAergic Neurons (Striatal-like)",
    "Microglia",
    "Excitatory Neurons (L6)", 
    "Excitatory Neurons (Deep Layers)"
  )
)

# Apply the mapping
for(i in 1:nrow(cluster_mapping)) {
  all_samples$cell_type[all_samples$seurat_clusters == cluster_mapping$cluster[i]] <- cluster_mapping$cell_type[i]
}

# Verify
table(all_samples$seurat_clusters, all_samples$cell_type)

# Optional: Convert to factor for better plotting
all_samples$cell_type <- factor(all_samples$cell_type, 
                               levels = c("Excitatory Neurons (L2-3)",
                                         "Excitatory Neurons (L4)",
                                         "Excitatory Neurons (L5)",
                                         "Excitatory Neurons (L6)",
                                         "Excitatory Neurons (Deep Layers)",
                                         "GABAergic Interneurons (Drd1/Drd2+)",
                                         "GABAergic Interneurons (Multiple)",
                                         "GABAergic Neurons (Striatal-like)",
                                         "Astrocytes",
                                         "Oligodendrocyte Precursor Cells",
                                         "Microglia",
                                         "Endothelial Cells",
                                         "Activated/Reactive Cells"))








# Once verified, continue with the analysis
# UMAP by cell type
DimPlot(all_samples, 
        group.by = "cell_type", 
        label = TRUE,
        repel = TRUE,
        raster = FALSE,
        label.size = 3) +
  ggtitle("Cell Type Classification") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))
```
```{r}


# First, create the spatial plot to get the exact colors
p_spatial <- ImageDimPlot(all_samples, 
             group.by = "cell_type",
             cols = 'glasbey',
             size = 0.5,
             dark.background = FALSE) +
  ggtitle("Spatial Distribution of Cell Types") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"))

# Print to see the colors
print(p_spatial)

# Alternative: Manual glasbey-like colors (similar to what you see in spatial plot)
n_cell_types <- length(levels(all_samples$cell_type))
glasbey_like_colors <- c(
  "#1F77B4", "#FF7F0E", "#2CA02C", "#D62728", "#9467BD",
  "#8C564B", "#E377C2", "#7F7F7F", "#BCBD22", "#17BECF",
  "#FF9896", "#AEC7E8", "#FFBB78", "#C49C94", "#F7B6D2",
  "#C7C7C7", "#DBDB8D", "#9EDAE5", "#FFED6F", "#C5B0D5"
)[1:n_cell_types]
names(glasbey_like_colors) <- levels(all_samples$cell_type)

# Create UMAP with these colors
p_umap <- DimPlot(all_samples, 
        group.by = "cell_type", 
        label = TRUE,
        repel = TRUE,
        raster = FALSE,
        cols = glasbey_like_colors) +
  ggtitle("Cell Type Classification") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 16, face = "bold"))

print(p_umap)

# Save UMAP as PDF
ggsave("umap_cell_types_glasbey.pdf", 
       p_umap, 
       width = 12, 
       height = 8, 
       device = "pdf")
print("UMAP plot saved as 'umap_cell_types_glasbey.pdf'")

# Save spatial plot as PDF
ggsave("spatial_cell_types_glasbey.pdf", 
       p_spatial, 
       width = 12, 
       height = 8, 
       device = "pdf")
print("Spatial plot saved as 'spatial_cell_types_glasbey.pdf'")
```
```{r}
# List of genes to visualize
genes_of_interest <- c("Cux1", "Cux2", "Tbr1", "Fezf2", "Bcl11b", "Foxp2", "Gfap", "Rorb", "Ppp1r1b", "Pecam1")

# Check which genes are present in the dataset
available_genes <- genes_of_interest[genes_of_interest %in% rownames(all_samples)]
missing_genes <- genes_of_interest[!genes_of_interest %in% rownames(all_samples)]

cat("Available genes:", paste(available_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
if (length(missing_genes) > 0) {
  cat("Missing genes:", paste(missing_genes, collapse = ", "), "\n")
}

# Create plots for each available gene
for (gene in available_genes) {
  
  # UMAP expression plot
  p_umap <- FeaturePlot(all_samples, 
                        features = gene,
                        cols = c("lightgray", "red"),
                        raster = FALSE) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- UMAP Expression")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))
  
  # Spatial expression plot
  p_spatial <- ImageFeaturePlot(all_samples,
                               features = gene,
                               cols = c("lightgray", "red"),
                               size = 0.5) +
    ggtitle(paste(gene, "- Spatial Expression")) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"))
  
  # Print plots
  print(p_umap)
  print(p_spatial)
  
  # Save as PDF
  ggsave(paste0("umap_expression_", gene, ".pdf"), 
         p_umap, 
         width = 10, 
         height = 8, 
         device = "pdf")
  
  ggsave(paste0("spatial_expression_", gene, ".pdf"), 
         p_spatial, 
         width = 10, 
         height = 8, 
         device = "pdf")
  
  cat("Saved plots for:", gene, "\n")
}

# Optional: Create a summary with smaller plots for quick overview
cat("\n=== Creating Summary Grid ===\n")

# UMAP grid
umap_plots <- list()
for (gene in available_genes) {
  umap_plots[[gene]] <- FeaturePlot(all_samples, 
                                   features = gene,
                                   cols = c("lightgray", "red"),
                                   raster = FALSE) +
    ggtitle(gene) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 10))
}

# Arrange UMAP grid (if you have patchwork package)
if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {
  library(patchwork)
  umap_grid <- wrap_plots(umap_plots, ncol = 3) + 
    plot_annotation(title = "Gene Expression - UMAP Overview")
  print(umap_grid)
  ggsave("umap_expression_grid.pdf", umap_grid, width = 15, height = 12)
}

print("All gene expression plots saved as individual PDF files!")


```
```{r}

# Prepare the SCT assay for DE analysis
# Use normalized Xenium data for DE analysis (simpler and more reliable)
cat("Using normalized Xenium data for DE analysis...\n")

# Normalize the Xenium data
l23_neurons <- NormalizeData(l23_neurons, 
                            assay = "Xenium",
                            normalization.method = "LogNormalize",
                            scale.factor = 10000)

# Set Xenium as default assay
DefaultAssay(l23_neurons) <- "Xenium"

# Now run DE analysis
de_ld_vs_wt <- FindMarkers(l23_neurons,
                          ident.1 = "LD",
                          ident.2 = "WT",
                          group.by = "genotype",
                          features = available_q22_genes,
                          logfc.threshold = 0,
                          min.pct = 0.05)

print("DE results for 22q11 genes (LD vs WT) - Normalized Xenium:")
print(de_ld_vs_wt)

# Create bar plot
library(ggplot2)
de_ld_vs_wt$gene <- rownames(de_ld_vs_wt)
de_ld_vs_wt$direction <- ifelse(de_ld_vs_wt$avg_log2FC > 0, "Up in LD", "Down in LD")
de_ld_vs_wt$significant <- de_ld_vs_wt$p_val_adj < 0.05

# Sort by fold change
de_ld_vs_wt <- de_ld_vs_wt[order(de_ld_vs_wt$avg_log2FC), ]

ggplot(de_ld_vs_wt, aes(x = reorder(gene, avg_log2FC), y = avg_log2FC, 
                        fill = direction, alpha = significant)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = "22q11 Gene", y = "Log2 Fold Change (LD vs WT)", 
       title = "22q11 Genes in L2/3 Neurons - LD vs WT") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Down in LD" = "blue", "Up in LD" = "red")) +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c("TRUE" = 1, "FALSE" = 0.3)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# Save the plot
ggsave("22q11_genes_LD_vs_WT.pdf", width = 10, height = 8)

# Run all key comparisons
cat("\n=== Running All Key Comparisons ===\n")

comparisons <- list(
  "LD_vs_WT" = c("LD", "WT"),
  "LD_NAC_vs_LD" = c("LD_NAC", "LD"),
  "WT_NAC_vs_WT" = c("WT_NAC", "WT"),
  "LD_NAC_vs_WT_NAC" = c("LD_NAC", "WT_NAC")
)

de_results <- list()

for (comp_name in names(comparisons)) {
  ident.1 <- comparisons[[comp_name]][1]
  ident.2 <- comparisons[[comp_name]][2]
  
  cat("Running:", comp_name, "\n")
  
  de_results[[comp_name]] <- FindMarkers(l23_neurons,
                                        ident.1 = ident.1,
                                        ident.2 = ident.2,
                                        group.by = "genotype",
                                        features = available_q22_genes,
                                        logfc.threshold = 0,
                                        min.pct = 0.05)
}

# Print key results
cat("\n=== LD vs WT (Main Comparison) ===\n")
print(de_results$LD_vs_WT)

cat("\n=== LD_NAC vs LD (NAC Rescue) ===\n")
print(de_results$LD_NAC_vs_LD)

cat("\n=== WT_NAC vs WT (NAC Control) ===\n")
print(de_results$WT_NAC_vs_WT)

# Create summary table
summary_table <- data.frame(Gene = available_q22_genes)

for (comp_name in names(comparisons)) {
  summary_table[[paste0(comp_name, "_log2FC")]] <- NA
  summary_table[[paste0(comp_name, "_padj")]] <- NA
  
  for (gene in available_q22_genes) {
    if (gene %in% rownames(de_results[[comp_name]])) {
      summary_table[summary_table$Gene == gene, paste0(comp_name, "_log2FC")] <- 
        de_results[[comp_name]][gene, "avg_log2FC"]
      summary_table[summary_table$Gene == gene, paste0(comp_name, "_padj")] <- 
        de_results[[comp_name]][gene, "p_val_adj"]
    }
  }
}

print("Summary Table of 22q11 Gene Expression:")
print(summary_table)

# Save results
write.csv(summary_table, "22q11_genes_DE_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\nResults saved to '22q11_genes_DE_summary.csv'\n")

```
